title: Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent
description: Great for festivals and car camping, the Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent sets up in as little as 10 seconds, letting you get back to the fun. This camping tent is uniquely designed with preassembled poles, allowing the tent to pop into place in a snap. The taped floor seams keep you dry even when the ground is soaking wet, while the multi-position rainfly allows you to find the perfect balance of protection and ventilation. This instant tent offers room for two camp pads and comes with two integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. This 2-person tent folds flat for simple storage and is lightweight for easy portability.
reviewText: Bought this for my daughter. It was an excellent choice. Taking it down took a few tries but she has now mastered it.

title: Coleman 12-In. Steel Tent Stakes
description: You'll sleep soundly knowing your tent is secured with Coleman 12-In. Steel Tent Stakes. These heavy-duty stakes are specially-designed to break through some of the toughest ground.
reviewText: These are junk. They tore up my rubber mallet when hammering them in. They also bent when trying to hammer them into the Black Rock City Playa. They never came out under decent wind but they bent so bad during removal that I had to throw them all away after the first and only use :(

title: Coleman Folding Double Wash Basin
reviewText: This would be good for backpack camping but not sturdy enough for car camping. Found the unit flimsy and it tipped over on my daughter while she was washing. Spilled right down her legs. It's hard enough to get a 13  year old to wash dishes without having to worry about this. I washed a sharp knife and had to be too careful not to poke the vinyl.

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText:
It's a decent enough grill.  Really lacks any control over flame.  High might as well be "Volcano" and low might as well be "Afterburner"

That's fine.. my first cook on it went ok, once I got a feel for it, the second was better.

What kills me is the migraine inducing rattle when the thing is in a car being moved.  The grill plates rattle, the wheels rattle, the lid rattles..Fine..  I remove the plates, bungee the lid down, and tape the wheels in place.

Then the hinge hardware rattles too.  And it's all tightened down.  Sigh.  Call Coleman, it's normal for it to do that.

So, it's in the box.. Waiting for UPS to pick it up.

title: Coleman QuickBed Elite Extra High Airbed – Queen
description: Featuring an extra-tall design for added cushioning, the Coleman QuickBed Elite Extra High Airbed provides a comfortable nights sleep wherever you are. Designed for both indoor and outdoor use, this inflatable mattress has an integrated 4D pump for easily inflating the bed at home or on the go (batteries sold separately). The soft plush top offers a velvet-like sleeping surface, while the ComfortStrong coil construction provides better support and superior comfort, so you wake up feeling rested. Factory tested to be leak-free, the air bed features an AirTight system and Double Lock valve that help keep the mattress at the perfect firmness level. This queen air mattress supports up to 600 pounds and fits standard queen size sheets. It comes with a carry bag for easy storage and portability.
reviewText:
Born a 'belly' in the middle of my bed. I do not recommend.

I bought mine at Target.

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
reviewText: Experienced camper. Bought the tent for casual car camping in temperate weather. Set it up last night.  There was some wind.  Nothing epic.  Nothing that isn't within the norm of "peak winds for any given week."  Not part of a storm system.  Within 2 hours one of the rings that anchors the tent floor to the poles and the ground had pulled out of the tent.  Had to spend the night in the back seat of the car.  When I awoke in the morning, all four rings had pulled out.  The pegs (with the rings still in them) were still attached to the ground, but my tent was stuck in a bush 20' away.  This wasn't an old/abused tent.  This was a tent I literally pulled out of the box to set it up.  What a complete waste of money.  The tent was so far away from actually performing that I cannot believe this was just one that slipped through the QA cracks.  This was simply cheap crap.  ...And the last Coleman tent I will ever buy.

title: Coleman 8D Square Pack-Away Full Size Lantern
description: Designed for convenience, the Coleman 8D Pack-Away Family Size Lantern features a clever telescoping design. Packed up, the lantern creates a built-in carry case for compact and easy storage and transport. Simply pull up the top to unpack the lantern to its full size (12" tall). The lantern's 15-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube provides an extra-bright, white light, with a reflector that directs light upward and outward for more efficient illumination -- it sheds an impressive 390 lumens of light on High. The light automatically turns off when the lantern is closed, to conserve your batteries. The lantern's bail handle is convenient for comfortable carrying or for hanging. The 8D Pack-Away Lantern runs for up to 24 hours on low or 12 hours on high, with a single set of eight D batteries (sold separately). The bulb and battery are easy to access and replace. Includes a limited 5-year warranty. Lantern measures approximately 12.13" x 6.81" x 6.57" extended, and weighs 2.58 pounds without batteries.
reviewText: This little portable is a neat design and stores away well when traveling.  I didn't give it a higher rating because there are other lights that require 8 batteries that give off more light and cost less.  Of course, they are LED not fluorescent, which is becoming pass.  This is a very good lighting unit but it has been surpassed with more light emitting LED models.

title: Coleman Lantern Carry Case
description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough case helps protect against dents, scratches and broken globes. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool, place it on the base and place the lid on top and lock it, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
reviewText: Exactly as described. Fit my 40 year old lantern like a glove. Can finally get rid of the original cardboard box.

title: Coleman Trekking Pole
description: Walk in comfort on your next hike with a Coleman Trekking Pole. Use it when you need it and put it in your backpack when you don't. Its easy-carry, easy-store aluminum design easily extends from 27 to 53 in., and the contoured cork handle with adjustable nylon strap makes it the perfect walking companion. With interchangeable tips, it will get you through any terrain.
reviewText: Great quality for the price.  Has already see its way through the hiking trails of a few state parks; no complaints here.  Would definitely buy again.

title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: First new cooler leaked from the drain spout.  Second replacement cooler - leaked from the drain spout.  The first one leaked around the gasket.  The second one leaked around the cap - which is not tight enough or fitted properly. Two new ones, both defective - you decide if you want to buy this product

title: Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set, Red
description:
Serve up your favorite foods around the campfire or portable stove with the Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set. Made of double-fired enamel and featuring a timeless red speckled design, this outdoor dinnerware set offers classic style and durability and is dishwasher safe for easy cleaning. This 24-piece set includes four 10-inch dinner plates, four 12-ounce coffee mugs with handles, and four 6-inch bowls, making it a great choice for family meals at picnics, BBQs, and campsites. It also includes a silverware kit with four plastic-handled forks, spoons, and knives stored in a convenient roll-up pouch for easy organization and storage. Ideal for camping, picnicking, and even backyard use, this set of Coleman speckled dinnerware includes everything you need to eat a meal under the stars. The 24-piece set is outfitted with four 10-inch dinner plates, four 10-ounce mugs, and four 6-inch bowls, giving you and three co-campers enough containers for a main course, a side salad, and an after-dinner hot chocolate. In addition, the set comes with four knives, four spoons, and four forks, so you don't need to rely on plastic silverware when eating. Best of all, the set is durable and dishwasher-safe, with a red-speckled enamel surface that holds up to heavy use. The silverware also includes a compact carrying case to keep the cutlery bunched together. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText:
We have a set of Coleman enamel dinnerware from almost ten years ago.  We now have three more children who are just now old enough for a real camping trip all at once, and we needed more dishes.  My wife ordered these when they were on sale, so she figured that for $20 they couldn't be as bad as some of the reviews were saying.  Unfortunately, they are pretty awful.  There are clear bubbles and defects that you can readily see.  The metal isn't entirely covered and will likely need to be treated to stop the dishes from rusting further than they already appear to be around the edges.  I'm not sure if we are going to keep these or not.  It mostly depends on whether we can find a better substitute.  By comparison, our original dishes look brand new next to these. No rusting, not flaking, no flaws.

I'm really disappointed in this brand that I've trusted for years with so many different camping products.

title: Coleman Xtreme Cooler, 28-Quart, Dark Gray/Orange/Light Gray Overmold
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time, and make the fun last longer, when you bring a Coleman 28 Quart Xtreme Cooler. Xtreme technology uses an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls to keep your items cold for up to 3 days. Large enough to hold 36 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for the entire party. Whether you're headed on a long weekend camping trip, a roadtrip, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip no-crush handles make getting to your destination easy and comfortable. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid will keep your cold drink close while you enjoy the day.
reviewText:
I have mixed feelings about this cooler.  I have a big one and love it, but wanted a smaller one for weekend campouts so i was excited about a 28 Quart.  First the good,  I LOVE the size, and holding a 2 litter great.  I freeze 3 or 4 full of water and it keeps everything cool without getting wet all weekend. 3 days plus and I still have ice that way, and you can drink the cool water if need be. My pic is on day 3.

The bad.  The handle is CRAP. Complete crap! Someone thought a single handle with a soft grip would be nice.  To get the grip, they carve out over HALF of the plastic and destroy the structural integrity of the handle.  It flexes in the middle under weight and i'm sure it will break eventually.  This cooler deserves two side handles like the other Xtremes! Coleman fix it!!!  In the same size Marine cooler they left the handle solid and its much stronger.

title: Coleman Compression Sack
description: Whether you're packing or storing your gear, reduce your bulk with a Coleman Compression Sack. Its unique design compresses large sleeping bags or other soft items with three straps that tighten around the outside. When you're ready to go, the sturdy web handle on the bottom makes one-handed carrying simple.
reviewText: it is ok

title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.
reviewText:
The rainfly leaked in the seam though the shower was not heavy. I will probably return it.
I owned 2 previous Coleman tent (6 people) and a screen but they are OK.

title: Coleman Triton Series InstaStart 2-Burner Stove
description: Cook breakfast, lunch and dinner on the legendary durability of a Coleman Triton Series InstaStart 2-Burner Stove. The InstaStart push-button matchless lighting system makes ignition easy. Once this propane camping stove starts to heat up, PerfectHeat Technology gets the temperatures just right, WindBlock Shields keep the wind from blowing the flames around and the PerfectFlow Pressure Control System keeps the heat steady on each of the high-performance 11,000-BTU burners. The spacious cooking surface is large enough to fit a 10-in. (25.4 cm) pan and 12-in. (30.48 cm) pan at the same time, and independently-adjustable controls allow you to set your heat anywhere from simmer to boil. The two burner camp stove is also efficient, with a 1-hr. runtime on high using a single 16.4-oz. propane cylinder. When the fun is done, removable heavy-duty, nickel-chrome grates make cleaning easy, and the built-in handle will have you carrying this lightweight stove effortlessly.
reviewText: Works great and worked every time. I had no problem with the igniter and had no issues with the control knob sensitivity. The cover design blocked the wind and the stove top was easy to clean.

title: Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair
description: Bring a comfortable seat with you to the campsite with the Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair. Ideal for camping, picnics, or backyard barbeques, this versatile folding chair offers premium comfort in a roomy yet portable design. It features a breathable mesh back that allows the breeze to cool your back on hot days, plus an integrated mesh cup holder that keeps a refreshing beverage within your reach. Designed to ensure your comfort, the chair features an extra-wide 21.3-inch seat that offers plenty of room to lounge and adjustable arms that let you customize the chair to suit your preferences. The strong steel frame can support up to 250 pounds and withstands seasons of heavy use. And thanks to its collapsible design and convenient carrying bag, this folding chair is easy to store and transport.
reviewText: Great economical camp chair pretty light.

title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
reviewText: The product was in poor condition and did not look new as advertised/purchased.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText: Tabs ripped off the first time we set it up.  I would say outs a nice tent otherwise, but I'm worried about the quality of the rest of the tent.

title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
reviewText: Nice looking cooler.  I had it latched and forgot and gently pulled up the lid, and the entire locking attachment on the inside came out.  Pretty sure thats not supposed to happen.  I was always a true beliver in Coleman products, but after this last camping trip....not so much.

title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
reviewText: we ordered both green and blue.  It is as described.  We use it daily.  Great so far.

title: Coleman Folding Double Wash Basin
reviewText: A bit small, but does the job

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText:
It's a ten-dollar hatchet sold at department stores. This same hatchet is also marketed by Coghlan's, Ozark Trail, Land's End, and many others. They're all the same China-made hatchet, except the branding.

The hatchet is 13" long, and except for the rubber grip cover, is all steel. The handle is solid tubular steel, and it goes all the way through the rubber handle cover. The rubber cover is black and slightly oval-shaped, a welcome addition to the circular handle. The handle cover is 6-1/2" long and has a slight axe handle shape, as opposed to a straight channel. A hard plastic disc is molded on to the handle to serve as (I think) overstrike protection. It's not attached to anything, it's just formed around the handle, and will slide around after the first few strikes. If you want to add a lanyard hole, you can drill one into the rubber handle cover. Go through the rear part and you'll avoid drilling into the steel handle. If you leave this hatchet in the rain, or even outside overnight, it will rust.

The head is attached to the handle with a metal fusion. It's drop-forged 1045 carbon steel and weighs 1 pound, 4 ounces. I tested mine at 47 on the Rockwell scale. The edge is 3" top-to-bottom, and has a flattened butt for driving stakes or hammering. The entire head, except for the edge, is painted black. The edge is absolutely horrible; it's too high. I had to buy a 40-grit belt for my 1" X 30" sander to get the edge angle right, as all the other belts I had were too fine. With the edge angle established, final sharpening was easy. The hatchet will take a razor-sharp edge, but the steel is too soft to hold it for long. For splitting wood, it's fine. The head hasn't come loose at all. There's also a small nail removal notch, but it's pretty much worthless. To use it, the nail has to sticking out by at least 1/4", and the opening slot is only 1/8" at its widest.

This is typical of a department store hatchet for ten dollars. It's serviceable once you grind the edge right and do some basic rust prevention. If you want a better hatchet for $10, buy a used one at a flea market or yard sale and bring it back to life yourself. This Coleman hatchet is okay for the average homeowner, but is hardly worth mention as a serious tool.

title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.
reviewText: straps ripped easily

title: Coleman Folding Shovel with Pick
description: Coleman 2000016390 shovel folding w/pick. Camping knives & tools shovels. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping knives & tools shovels
reviewText: Cheap,  not very strong

title: Coleman PowerChill 3000001497 Thermoelectric Cooler with Power Supply (40-Quart), Silver
description:
Perfect for the office, dorm, RV, truck or car, the Coleman PowerChill Thermoelectric Cooler is fully portable, plugging into your vehicle's cigarette lighter. The PowerChill's new motor design provides quiet, long-lasting performance, cooling the contents down to 40-degreeF BELOW the surrounding temperature. The PowerChill is designed with many convenient features, including a patented, adjustable door that can open on the right or left side. The cooler can be used in two positions, either as an upright or a chest cooler. An adjustable shelf divider helps keep things organized inside. The PowerChill Cooler's molded handles make it easy to lift and carry. With a 40-quart interior that holds up to 44 cans of soda or beer in addition to snacks, the Coleman portable PowerChill thermoelectric cooler is ideal for the office, dorm, RV, truck, or car. The cooler is compatible with any vehicle's cigarette lighter. As a result, you can bring the PowerChill pretty much anywhere, from the campsite to the tailgater to your backyard BBQ. The PowerChill is equipped with a redesigned motor that provides quiet, long-lasting performance. The motor cools contents down to 40 degrees F below the surrounding temperature, so you don't have to fret once the ice starts melting in your standard cooler. The PowerChill includes several handy features, including a patented adjustable door that can open on both the right and left side, an adjustable shelf divider to keep things organized, and molded handles that make the cooler easy to lift and carry. The PowerChill even functions in two positions: upright or chest style. The PowerChill measures 15 by 21.75 by 17.125 inches (W x H x D). About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: The cooler needs supporting ice packs to cool to a safe refrigeration level. I keep an thermometer in it, and without ice support, it doesn't cool below 57 degrees - even when run all day.

title: Coleman Camp Oven
description:
Bring the joy of fresh-baked muffins, cinnamon rolls, cornbread, and other treats to any campsite with the Coleman Camp Oven. Designed to fit neatly on Coleman propane and liquid fuel camp stoves, this portable oven gives you the ability to bake treats or keep food warm while spending time outdoors. The included interior rack adjusts to 3 heights, while the easy-to-read thermometer lets you bake with confidence. This portable oven is made from smooth aluminized steel that resists corrosion and scratches and wipes clean easily, and it folds flat for convenient carrying and storage when its time to pack up camp. Camping stoves and other cooking accessories have made it possible to cook most things outdoors, but baked goods have always been out of the question. Until now, that is. Made of smooth aluminized steel, the Coleman camping oven lets you bake a batch of muffins, biscuits, or rolls right at the campsite.


The Coleman camping oven is designed to sit on a two- or three-burner camping stove.
The oven doesn't require electric or gas power to work. Instead, campers simply place the oven on top of a Coleman two- or three-burner stove, which provides all the heat you need to bake your favorite dishes. The oven also offers such baking essentials as an adjustable steel bake rack and an easy-to-read thermometer. And when you're done baking, the oven folds flat for easy storage. Made in China, the Coleman camping oven measures 12 by 12 by 12 inches (W x H x D) when standing and weighs 7 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: A good product AFTER you CUT & FIT two Pizza Stones on the bottom and middle rack to help it maintain a constant temperature.

title: Coleman Beach Shade
description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
reviewText:
We own both this Coleman Road Trip Beach Shade and the SportsBrella XL. We brought both down to the Oregon coast last weekend and tried them both in the beach environment (we normally use them for baseball games :) )

Pros:
* The Coleman provides better sun protection then the SportsBrella XL as it has a greater depth an no pole in the middle. More people can fit under it comfortably.
* The Coleman packs up nice and tight and can fit anywhere, the SportsBrellaXL would not have fit in our car (mini-van was fine going through the middle seats)

Cons:
* The Coleman was absolute hell to get setup with moderate winds coming off the Pacific, and it's flexible frame made it constantly move and bend in the wind. In contrast the SportsBrellaXL was very easy to setup.
* Likewise the Coleman was hell to tear down and packup with the wind, where as the SportsBrella was one button press.
* I originally though the floor of the Coleman would be a benefit, but honestly with small kids within 30 minutes the whole inside was filled with sand.

title: Coleman 12-Piece Stainless Steel Enamel Flatware Set
description:
The Coleman 12-Piece Stainless Steel Enamel Flatware Set contains 4 spoons, 4 forks and 4 knives. Made of durable stainless steel with decorative enamel handles, this Flatware Set is easy to clean and dishwasher-safe. The set includes a convenient carry case, so it's easy to pack and transport. Perfect for family camping trips, picnics in the park, weekends at the cabin, and extra cutlery for the boat, RV, or backyard barbecues, this stainless steel flatware set from Coleman includes service for four (four forks, knives, and spoons). It also includes a handy carrying case that easily rolls up for compact storage (and easy packing). About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText:
Do know that this flatware may not be red once delivered, mine was black.  If that matters to you at all but mine was to match my blue enamel dining set while camping and I am a "Glamper" so it matters to me.  I design camping photos for publication. The flatware goes back!

Update June 15, 2012.
The Seller (through Amazon) said they will not pay for the return of the black flatware because it's Amazon's fault it is pictured red. So not only did GoCampingCheap charge me $8.00 to ship it to me but they also made me pay $8.13 to send it back.  The flatware only cost $12.99.  I paid $16.00 in shipping charges total on a $13.00 purchased.  Do not purchase from Standard Camping Supplies or GoCampingCheap (same company)or you will regret it if the product is delivered wrong or if you don't like the product accordinng to them.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: this axe is absolutely garbage out of the box. but if you spend the time to sharpen it, it woks very well. it has a very nice weight to it. perfect for camping or hiking. so if you sharpen it it'll be good. but also it takes a while to sharpen, probably a good hour or 2. the steel isn't that good either, so youll need to touch up your blade a little more often.

title: Coleman Sundome Dome Tent
reviewText: Tent works good

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText:
It's a Coleman.  Great entry-level tent that's easy to put up, even for a 10-year old.  Love the bathtub floor (I sure wish tents had that feature when I was a kid, it would have saved me many nights of gear getting soaking wet).  I also love that the pole sleeves are as small as possible and that it relies on pole clips for most of the length of the poles.  That makes getting the poles in and up so much easier than a full-length sleeve.  I wouldn't exactly call them snag-proof but they're a notable improvement over full-length sleeves.

I like the fly on this model, it has one pole that goes front-to-back and does not require much bending.  Similar Coleman models have a smaller fly pole that you have to bend into a U-shape, putting tremendous tension on the fiberglass and subjecting it to splintering.  This model's fly pole is thicker and only has a slight bend so I expect it to last longer.

Biggest complaint is the zipper gets caught in the "zipper cover" all the time and for kids who don't know any better, they just keep pulling until it's completely jammed.  This is not a great design and needs to be revisited.

As with all tents on the market, "4-person" means 4 people sleeping right up on each other with no room for gear.  So this is really a tent for 3 people at most.  We've slept 3 in it comfortably (1 adult/2 kids, 2 adults/1 kid) although we did start to run short on room for gear at some point.

title: Coleman Quickbed
description: Get a restful nights sleep in your home or tent with a Coleman QuickBed. Relax on 32 comfort coils that provide superior support while the soft top offers luxurious comfort for sleepers on the go. With durable PVC construction and the Airtight System, your airbed is guaranteed to keep its customized firmness all night long. The integrated Wrap 'N' Roll Storage System allows you to fold, roll and store the airbed quicklymaking cleanup easy. W.C. Coleman was a young salesman taking a stroll after a hard days work selling typewriters when he spotted a new type of lamplight in a drugstore window in Brockton, Alabama. This new light burned with a strong, steady white flame and was fueled by gasoline. The standard lamp of the era burned kerosene and produced a smoky, flickering, yellowish light. W.C. was stricken with very poor eyesight, and was very interested in this new, steady white light that enabled him to read even the smallest print in books and on medicine bottles. Coleman saw potential in the new light, and through his vision a new company was born that would put Americas farms and ranches in a new light, and would eventually make his name synonymous with outdoor fun.
reviewText:
I took this bed on my last camping trip though I didn't buy this on Amazon.

I wish I had really checked it out (out of the box) before taking it up the mountains.  I bought a tent (please see my review) here on Amazon that stated that it would fit a Queen size air mattress, which is what the box stated it was.

Once the tent and mattress were set up I noticed that there was still half the floor left, not believing my camping partner that it was a full size matress I showed him the box that stated that it was a queen, later agreeing with him that it was indeed a full size put in the wrong box.

The matress itself (states in the instructions) loses air due to the fabric stretching, we had to refill (top it off) each night (slightly irritating).  The feel of the mattress itself reminds you of a suade type of material.  The "wells" (for lack of a better word) in the top of it are a bit uncomfortable (I would recommend sleeping on something else on top of the matress (we used sheets).  After getting home we used a vacuumn to clean of any debris left behind, we also tried to clean off marks with dawn and wet rag, sorry to say it didn't clean up well.  Once dried thoroughly we tried to roll it up into it's "roll up material" , it didn't center well to roll it per the store away instructions nor did the ties match up well for perfect tieing.  We ended up doing the best we could but also storing it in a plastic container.

All in all, it was better than sleeping on the ground, but not really big enough for two adults comfortably.

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: Perfect size for my sons baseball games. Enough for drinks and lunch. Strap is great!

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: I saw these last night at a camping goods store and am ordering this one online now.  I wanted to put in a note to tell anyone that is unsure about the sizing that the stove we have recommends this model number for the carry bag (recommended on the box under the flap on one of the sides), but ours is not the grill type one as shown above.  I can see why people are confused.  I suggest you look at the box of the grill/stove you buy and see which bag they recommend you buy to get the right size.  Our stove is #2000007502 Perfectflow Stove.  These look to be very sturdy and will be helpful to transport the stove.  I will update if the one recommended doesn't fit or is not the same as we examined last night.  Update:  It fit perfectly with room to spare and is very well made.  My only problem is that I accidentally bought two of them and only need one.  Guess I will have to send one back.

title: Coleman Tent Kit
description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
reviewText: nice kit. compact an nice carry bag for keeping everything together.will work nicely for cleaning the Hemlock needles out of the inside of the tent.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: This axe is cheap yes but not worth it. The axe feels cheap and is made cheap. My axe came with rust on it and chips and notches in the blade just save your money and buy a better axe for a little more money! I have a Fiskar axe and hatchet now and they are way better products might cost a little more but worth it!

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText:
Not your Dad's cooler, that's for sure.

Needed something for the gym that would keep my post-workout meal cool here in Vegas. This cooler is garbage and it cheaply made. The inside hard plastic liner is PAPER THIN - not THICK like the old school ones used to be. The material on the outside doesn't insulate well enough to keep anything cool on the inside. The zipper on top to seal the whole thing, after just a few weeks of use, is already getting stuck when trying to close. The strap (which I'm about to just cut off with scissors is annoying) is actually stitched on - you can't remove it! What the hell?

What's happened to Coleman over the years? They used to produce quality stuff. Too much outsourcing to China apparently to bring the cost down.

Anyways, don't waste your money or time. I'm the hunt for a new one now.

title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. Keep flying insects out when hiking or working in mosquito- or gnat-ridden territory with the Coleman insect head net. The net is designed to fit comfortably over most headgear, and is made with a 1mm maximum netting grade.
reviewText:
I've been using this product almost every afternoon when I take about a 3 hour hike through the woods, and so far so good!

As long as you try to be diligent on not catching the head net on branches and stuff, this product should last you a great long while.

I would recommend this to anyone who is like me and prefers to not wear DEET bug sprays.

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
reviewText: Nice water carrier for camping or traveling. The only thing I don't like is the faucet. It is kind of small and fragile. My first one was broken after 2 years. .

title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText: Not comfortable at all for two people to sleep on. Used it once and then upgraded to a double high Intex mattress.

title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
reviewText: I was shopping around for a mess kit for 4 BOB's. I figured Colman big name associated I'm my book with quality. I was very disappointed with the quality. Some of the edges were a bit sharp. After doing research on mess kits I learned that it's the best idea to purchase aluminum mess kits. With the heat from the fire when heating liquid or food in the cup or pan the kit will warp or not be able to handle the big heat of a fire for cooking or warming. I'd spend the extra maybe 5 or 10.00 for good solid stainless steel kits.

title: Coleman Insta-Clip 2 #95 Mantles, Accordian Tube Shape - One Color, One Size
description: Coleman Insta-Clip 2 accordian tube mantles (#95). For use with Coleman fueled lantern models: 2000, 2500, 2555, 2600. Change mantles in seconds with tabbed clip attachment. Fits Coleman Northstar Lantern (Campmor No. 87575). 2 mantles per package.
reviewText: should have a better wire to attach to lamp

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText: Great unit. Lots of BTUs. Only problem is that the burners are so close to the grill, that heat distribution is difficult to manage when cooking multiple items.

title: Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad
description: Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.
reviewText: It'll work but the texture is not at all what I expected or wanted

title: Coleman Cooler Standard Drain Plug Assembly - 1" Shaft Length
reviewText: So if you're replacing your cooler drain assembly, this definitely fits.  The problem is that if your replacing the drain and the original drain assembly has a large nut on the interior to back up the gasket, this isn't the same.  Yes, it fits, but it's difficult to tighten in order to snug up the new gasket.  I haven't pressed this repair into service yet, so the jury is still out.  If you have a large hex key, you can tighten this assembly properly by placing the hex key on the interior fitting.  It's hollow (as it should be) but it is a large hex opening.  I don't know about you, but I've got all the tools I thought I would ever need, and I just don't have that large a hex key.  As far as I can tell, if you have a large enough SAE sized hex key you should be able to get this good and snug enough to compress the interior rubber washer.  I just didn't have one large enough.  I guess I just don't have enough tools.  Well, I'm off to buy more tools.  I hope this review was helpful.

title: Coleman Compact Propane Lantern
description:
Take along personal-sized light that lasts all night when you head outdoors with the Coleman Compact Propane Lantern. The compact design of this lantern provides a perfect amount of light for one person - up to 300 lumens - and saves space for other gear when you pack. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the chain handle allows for easy hanging. Meanwhile, the globe guard helps protect the globe from breaking. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. The Coleman 5132A700 One-mantle Compact Propane Lantern is the lantern that made camping cozy, at its easiest and most portable. Clean-burning propane fuel provides a soft glow for late-night stories or a beacon to guide you back to camp. This Coleman compact lantern lights with matches and is pressure-regulated for consistent light, regardless of weather. The porcelain ventilators will prevent rusting and help this lantern last you a long time. Powered by one 16.4 ounce propane cylinder (not included), this handy little lantern features slip-on mantles and a chain handle that can be used for carrying or hanging. Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText:
I've had a double mantle, and took my chance on the single mantle.  It's PERFECT for a family outing.  The nice thing that I use this one for is mounted on top of my propane tree.  It's an excellent light with good quality materials.

Pros:  Coleman quality.  Heavy gauge materials that look like they are going to be durable for years.  Easy to change mantle.  Compact storage.  Protective cage around glass globe.

Cons:  Little difficult to light, but with a sparker or a butane "match" it's actually moderately easy.  No durable storage case available.

This little lantern is almost perfect if you are space conscious (cars and SUV's are only getting smaller) but still want a fully funtional propane lantern.  One thing I did notice about this lantern, is that you can carry the lantern lit with the chain, you can feel warmth from the lantern, but not "heat".

The only thing that would make this lantern better, is if it had an "autolite" feature where when you turn the gas on, a little pizo ignighter sparks the thing to life.

title: Coleman Compact Propane Lantern
description:
Take along personal-sized light that lasts all night when you head outdoors with the Coleman Compact Propane Lantern. The compact design of this lantern provides a perfect amount of light for one person - up to 300 lumens - and saves space for other gear when you pack. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the chain handle allows for easy hanging. Meanwhile, the globe guard helps protect the globe from breaking. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. The Coleman 5132A700 One-mantle Compact Propane Lantern is the lantern that made camping cozy, at its easiest and most portable. Clean-burning propane fuel provides a soft glow for late-night stories or a beacon to guide you back to camp. This Coleman compact lantern lights with matches and is pressure-regulated for consistent light, regardless of weather. The porcelain ventilators will prevent rusting and help this lantern last you a long time. Powered by one 16.4 ounce propane cylinder (not included), this handy little lantern features slip-on mantles and a chain handle that can be used for carrying or hanging. Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText:
Do not buy this.  Total garbage.  I've had this out on five occasions.  The piezo ignition pretty much never worked.  I wish they were sold without them but I doubt it would fix my latest issue..  Changing the mantle is a already a hassle and with the igniter right next to it total pain in the ass.  I have yet to change the mantle without getting some tears.  The latest problem seems to be a fuel flow issue..  Don't know what the cause is.  I changed fuel bottles and changed the mantle but it did not do anything.  It burns like a candle now.  A blue flame that licks out of the globe.

Coleman used to be a solid brand...  When I moved on to more expensive gear I continued to use Coleman lanterns and stoves.  My next coleman lantern, and stove (not the time to go into that), I'll probably find at a garage sale.  The quality vintage stuff.

I've used it a couple of times now.  Except for the igniter it worked well.  The igniter seldom works. But it works better than it did before I pushed it closer to the mantle with my thumb.  Coleman might consider just leaving it off.  The globe vents well so when you light it by hand  there is no worrying about singing the hairs off your hand from a ball of flame.  The mantel must be completely intact.  Any tears will significantly impacts the light output.  Otherwise it is plenty bright doesn't use much fuel.  More importantly for me is how quiet it is even when turned all the way up.  If this had been just a lantern without an igniter I probably would have given it four stars.

After its performance on my last trip, the first after my initial review, I am dropping my rating from three to one star.  There seems to be an issue with the fuel flow that causes it to dim as if the tank was running dry which it wasn't.  I tried two other tanks thinking that might be the issue.  One of them was about three quarters full and the other brand new.  It started off fine but then it quickly dimmed again.  There was a small tear in the mantel so I replaced it thinking it might be the problem.  It wasn't.  Garbage.

title: Coleman 1-Gallon Jug
description: Keep enough ice-cold water to stay cool and hydrated on sweltering days with a Coleman 1 Gallon Beverage Cooler. The wide mouth top makes for easy filling, including adding ice by the handful. The flip spout makes grabbing a quick drink easy, and it won't snag open to better prevent spills. The bail handle makes carrying easy, and the screw-top lid stays secure, so no matter where you go you'll always have a refreshing drink ready for you.
reviewText: I bought this to use during the day while at work. I do like that it holds a gallon, which is more than enough for most people. I have not had any problems with it leaking, from what I can tell the seals are all still very tight. I have been using this jug for a little over a month now. My one issue with this product is that the lid is not insulated. The inside of the cooler is very  insulated, the walls look to be over an inch thick. The inner insulation, however, is somewhat lost by the lack of insulation on the lid. Because of this, the heat from the day can get into the cooler and warm up the contents rather quickly. The lid also "sweats" because of the lack of insulation, which makes it seem like the whole thing is leaking. Overall, it's a decent product, but nothing special. I would recommend something with an insulated lid if you are concerned with keeping things cold/hot.

title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
reviewText: Its about as expected.  I fit four teenage boys in this tent and they had enough room.  They really don't want to be anywhere near each other and NEED their space.  This was fine.  But sleep 8?  No.

title: Coleman Folding Double Wash Basin
reviewText: Started leaking almost immediately. Thin, cheap, useless.

title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText:
We needed extra chairs for our RV. These were well-rated and inexpensive.
Pros: They come in a carrying case and take up little storage space when folded.  They are more comfortable to sit in than our low-slung beach chairs that these replaced. Morning dew quickly dries off in the sun.
Cons: The drink pocket could be a little deeper and wider to handle a wine glass. The top of the back of the chair offers no support for the neck.
Bottom line: At this price, they're a deal!

title: Coleman Converta Cot
description: The Coleman Converta Cot provides comfortable seating at the campsite or in the backyard. Four back position and two foot positions for use as a lounge chair or cot.Unfolded Dimensions: 76 in. x 26 in. x 15.5 in..Folded Dimensions: 27.8 in. x 25 in. x 4.9 in..
reviewText: Not at all comfortable to sit in.  Since the middle part is level with the ground, your body just wants to slide down.  As a cot, it was alright.  Kind of low to the ground for my taste, and you can't really get it perfectly flat.  Worst of all though was the locking mechanism for the head rest.  It's hard to lock it in and unlock it.  Half the time the retaining bar just winds up popping off and you have to bend it back in place.

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText: This grill has been perfect for tailgating and camping. Like that I can easily clean the inside.  Transports well too.

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText: I was able to set it up by myself. The door opens on the side so it has to be tied to keep it out of the dirt.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Upon arrival I noticed a corner of the counter was chipped. Also, this thing does not fold up very well at all. For the price, I would not buy this again, or recommend it to anyone.

title: (2) COLEMAN Aluminum Camping Mess Kit Cookware Sets - Cup, Pan, Pot, & Plate
description: 1 Buy = 2 Coleman Aluminum Mess Kits! With the Coleman Aluminum Mess Kit you can fit all the cookware and dishes you need to make and enjoy a hearty meal in the size of a pie pan. Our engineers have designed a 7 in. (17.78 cm) frying pan, 16 oz. (473.18 ML)pot with lid, 6.5 in. (15.24 cm)deep dish plate and 8 oz. aluminum cup to nest perfectly together and snap shut for easy storage. The 5 piece Coleman Aluminum Mess Kit is durable and cleans easily. VMInnovations is an authorized Coleman dealer selling new, warranted products! Please see our other listings, as we have a wide variety of camping/outdoor products.
reviewText: Having been in Cub Scouts and Boy Scouts in my younger years, I had grown fond of these simple yet very dependable mess kits while on various outings. They are made of aluminum to save weight in your gear pack and hold up well to extended uses for trips last two to three weeks or longer. Easy to clean up and take up very little room in your gear pack. Saving space in your pack, as any outdoors person knows is a very welcomed advantage. I, for one, am really glad to see these mess kits still on the market.

title: Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole, 8-Foot
description: Bring new life to an old tent or canopy with the Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole. Compatible with most camping tents and canopies, this durable tent pole easily adjusts from 3 to 8 feet to offer sturdy support where needed. A friction lock secures the replacement pole at the desired height and ensures the pole will not budge when holding up a canopy, tent, or other shelter. Durable steel construction gives this pole the reliable strength to withstand the rigors of outdoor use, helping bring older tents back to their full potential.
reviewText: Was used to hold up a tarp for extra coverage during rainy camping trip. It performed well, but could have used the bases to hold steady.

title: Coleman Micro Quad LED Mini Lantern
