Also, very difficult to clean after cooking food that has grease, such as hamburgers. The grease gets all over in the bottom and there's no way to take out the bottom tray and clean it.

title: Coleman Tent Kit
description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
reviewText: As other reviewers have said, this mallet stinks. Literally. It smells awful. I'm not sensitive to smells so I thought I might not have an issue with it, but it's bad. I've had it sitting on my deck for three weeks and it's still off gassing. The rest of the components are convenient and nice to have. Coleman needs to replace that mallet though.

title: Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Wheeled Cooler
description: Perfect for any tailgate, barbecue, beach trip or campsite, the Coleman 50-quart Xtreme Cooler has more than enough space for everything you need. With a unique design, this cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles and large enough for up to 84 cans. Have a seat lidRust proof and leak resistantHolds 2-liter bottles uprightBrand: ColemanQuantity: One (1) coolerColor options: Blue/white or grey/orangeMaterials: PlasticDimensions: 22.6 inches long x 17.6 inches wide x 17.7 inches highModel: 3000001840
reviewText:
Does keep cool well over time but I have a big issue spending this much on a cooler and then discovering that rainwater comes in through the overhang on the lid, the seemingly tight seal, and into the cooler in copious amounts.  I gathered about 4 inches in one night of rain.
It looks impossible for water to do this from the appearance, but, there it was the next morning- a several inch pool inside.
Couldn't a good company with a long history of making these things learn to prevent this?

Don't trust it.  Expect it to fill with water.

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText:
I'm quite disappointed with this product. I expected more but found myself wanting something different. First, if you make coffee on your stove I hope you use a very very large cauldron... because a normal coffee pot will not work on this product. I purchased the swaptop stove grate for this, but the burner is just too wide for smaller items (the heat will just go around it).

The portable propane tanks last enough time for making breakfast and maybe dinner (you'll go through a can a day... (i was only cooking for 1 person).

I wanted flexibility... to be able to grill and use my cast-iron... but I'm thinking it's better to just go with one of the small campstoves instead of using this grill.

Don't get me wrong.. works okay for some things... but the heat distribution is going to mess with your mind if you're a avid griller...

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: Seams split first rattle out the gate.

title: Coleman Weathermaster II Screened 10 Person 16 X 10 Tent with Hinged Door and Autoroll Windows, 7 Feet Tall and Fits 4 Queen Air Beds
reviewText: This is a HEAVY  tent to move around and really ANNOYING to setup. I'm going to return it tomorrow.  I just purchased this tent on sale with high hopes. I need a tent that's large enough for my husband, myself two boys and a dog. Thank god I did test run setup in the back yard before our camping trip. The directions are clear and easy to follow, however you really need 2 people to assemble the tent. I didn't like the fact that the tent ceiling setup is separate from the walls. I much prefer a dome style tent that can be put up quickly and taken down easily. I am also not pleased with the quality. The bag that it comes had a zipper that kept jamming and a seam that was splitting. Not a good sign for the tent itself. And a total pain since you would rely on that outer bag to store & move the tent around. The REI Base Camp 6 by comparison was a cakewalk to set up and really good quality but is not quite large enough. I ordered the Kelty Discovery 8 man tent and hope that one works.

title: Coleman Cold Glow Cooler Light
description: You'll always be able to find your favorite drink when you light up your cooler with the Coleman cold glow cooler light. It easily installs on the underside of your cooler lid to provide 50 lumens of light. Once attached, you can set it to the auto-on Mode and the motion-sensing technology will turn on your light when you open the lid and turn it off when you close the lid. Perfectly designed for use inside your cooler, it's water-resistant and the LEDs run cool so they won't contribute to ice melt.
reviewText: Battery came out during the ride , and the light got wet , and does not work after the first trip .

title: Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner
description:
The Coleman Stratus Adult Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner doubles as both a warm, cozy liner and a low-profile sleeping bag. On its own, the liner can keep you warm in temperatures as low as 50 degrees Fahrenheit. When placed inside another sleeping bag, it can provide 12 degrees Fahrenheit of extra thermal protection. This low-profile sleeping bag liner features a two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system that plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. It also features an elastic storage strap and stuff sack for easy packing and transport. The Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches in height. It is machine washable for easy cleaning. The Coleman 8197C410 Stratus Fleece Sleeping Bag Liner is the perfect accompaniment to any sleeping bag, especially when you are camping where you are not sure how cold it is going to be. This sleeping-bag liner is full-size, measuring 33-inches by 75-inches, and fits most full-size sleeping bags up to 5-feet 11-inches long. When used with a sleeping bag, this liner adds 12 degrees of thermal protection, making a 35-degree sleeping bag usable at temperatures as low as 23 degrees! The Coleman fleece sleeping-bag liner is commercial-machine-washable only and comes with its own stuff sack. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty
reviewText: It arrived, I tried to unzip it, the zipper WOULD NOT MOVE AN INCH. Correction; not a millimeter. Even had another person try to pull it while I held the bag - nothing. I've never seen anything like it, it's like the zipper is welded in place. Extremely disappointing for a Coleman product, was forced to return it immediately.

title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
reviewText: For emergencies.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: Side pocket lining ripped first time I put in a propane mini tank - other side was fine. Glad I got it for the grill/stove. I got it when the price dropped by 50% in the middle of the night (thanks, insomnia) - to justify the luxury. Is definitely convenient.

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. Great for camping in warm weather, this backpacking tent is designed with large windows and a ground vent to help push warm air up and out to keep you comfortable. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText: Very disappointed in the quality of this tent. The zipper broke almost immediately. I could zip one way, but if I zipped the other way, it came apart. I continued to use it for a while (zipping completely one way), then on my last trip, it broke and would not zip either way. Very embarrassing to get dressed in a tent with the door gaping open! Came home and purchased a different brand. I'm crossing my fingers it will hold up better because I've had it with the Coleman brand. What used to be the quality name in camping has really let me down.

title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
reviewText: everything sticks to it, but its light weight and sturdy as hell with together, by pack is 100+ lbs and it gets dropped and kicked around with these on the outside, still holding up. i suppose the only reason for 3 stars is because its just cheap materials but hey, it works

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: Purchased this, thinking I would be able to do some small chopping. I wasn't looking to split logs but essentially cut down firewood into small pieces to start a fire. No way. The blade is worthless. I suppose I could get it sharpened but based on other reviews after I got back from camping I'm just going to return it. In addition to the bad blade, the axe came with a slightly crooked handle which made it awkward to hold. If I was left-handed it may be beneficial because the curve is in that direction however since I'm right handed it's an opposite curve.

title: Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern
description: Make packing up your lantern as easy as possible with the Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 22 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.
reviewText: Pain to initially unpack and the enclosure doesn't really stay closed well. The handle pops off a lil too easily. But it is real easy to use and very very bright.

title: Coleman Dunk Bag
description: Make dish storage easy with a Coleman Dunk Bag. This breathable, multi-use bag is also designed to conveniently carry and store beach gear, bath toys or laundry. Constructed of mildew- and rot-resistant fabric, count on this bag to be with you trip after trip.
reviewText: I have not used this bag out in the field yet but they seem to be sturdy and are the perfect size for just about anything.

title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
reviewText:
We bought ours at Big 5 - it was yellow. This appears to be the same with instant set-up and 6-person capacity.

Our first mistake was not trying to assemble it at home before taking it into the wilderness. We bought it for the instant setup, as my husband was doing this solo while I arrived on a later boat.

One minute turned into THREE HOURS of setup due to poor Quality Control at the factory. Some of the pieces that were supposed to be together weren't, so he couldn't set it up in a minute or two as promised. He's an engineer, so he figured out the problem fairly quickly but not the solution. Two poles were not joined where they should have been. And when he did attempt to join them, he didn't do it right at first. (This is not a simple old-school join poles together problem.) He finally figured it out and raised the tent, but a screw did pop out and he couldn't put it back in where it came from, because it was broken and wouldn't stay.

Then, a mild gust brought the entire tent down. This is a design flaw, part of the joints that make the tent an instant-set tent. If it blows slightly the wrong way, the whole thing collapses. This happened to us twice. Our only saving grace was that the mild gusts happened in the afternoon and not late at night.

One other mentioned that the tent is hard to zip around the corners. I agree. Pretty flimsy.

I am extremely disappointed. Coleman used to make good tents.

title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: The classic stove. I love it. My parents had one while I was growing up and they still have it 30 years later. We used ours on our recent camping trip and had no problems with it. It lights easy, folds out and back down easily, and wind was not a problem. We used it to cook every meal and were totally satisfied.

title: Coleman High Stand,20.25" H x 18.75" W x 28" L
description: Coleman 2000020280 High Stand. Camping Furniture. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping Furniture
reviewText: I've used these stands for several years now for both my camping grill and stove. When all is said and done, they do work, but you have to be very careful when using them. On the plus side, they're very lightweight, pack up small, and are adjustable. The problem is they are also a bit flimsy. With no back or tabletop, your grill or stove rests gingerly across two thin beams. With the lid open and a propane tank attached, most grills and stoves are already a far cry from balanced. Toss in a rickety stand and some uneven ground and you've got a very hot balancing act on your hands. A modest bump would be enough to send your stove or grill crashing to the ground. I've had a few close calls and I'm generally pretty careful. Had my grill fall once before it was lit. Would recommend it only for those times where portability matters and there's nothing sturdier available to work with. Have since bought a Coleman Pack-Away Kitchen. It's not perfect, but the beams are definitely more secure and stable.

title: Coleman 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler, Blue
description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 2 days when you roll in with a Coleman 16 Quart Performance Wheeled Cooler. Large enough to hold 22 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for several people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or across the ball field, the recessed wheels and long, telescoping handle let you roll up with hardly any effort. The additional molded handles make it easy to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Coleman provides a convenient way to keep things cold, even when you're on-the-go, with the 16-Quart Personal Wheeled Cooler. This convenient cooler can hold up to 22 12-ounce cans or two 2-liter bottles upright and features traditional Coleman construction for rugged durability. Other features include an extendable handle that retracts for convenient storage and recessed wheels for easy transport to and from your favorite sporting events and outdoor activities. This durable Coleman cooler comes with a limited manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: The handle would not go all the way up and when you put it down it would go through the bottom and stick out... Very upset about this product...

title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText: abused one for 15+ years until it rusted out, just ordered another!!!

title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
reviewText: The first time we used this the plastic filter basket melted!  We followed the instructions exactly.  It was used outside, on a Coleman stove, plenty of ventilation, and the outside temperature was even in the low 50's.  Still the whole side of the plastic filter basket melted and stuck to the lid of the coffee pot.  Good thing we were paying attention to it before it caught anything on fire.

title: Coleman 16-Piece Cutlery Set
description: Coleman 2000015163 utensils 16 pc plastic. Camping cooking supplies cookware. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping cooking supplies cookware
reviewText: These are light functional cutlery which work fine. The price is is higher than I recently found at my Big-5 store, but still okay with my purchase. Will be using these for backpacking.

title: Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+
description: The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ offers both a grill and a stove for versatile campsite cooking. This durable steel-crafted outdoor appliance has a stove surface that fits a 10-inch pan as well as a 130-square inch grill area, letting you saut veggies while you're grilling chicken, or get the soup going while you're cooking up burgers. Each surface has independent temperature controls to give you precise control. The InstaStart push-button ignition powers up the portable grill with a simple press of a button with no matches needed, while integrated Wind Block panels help shield the flame from wind and can be folded down on calm days to be used as side tables. This outdoor grill features PerfectFlow pressure control technology to keep the heat steady even in extreme conditions and PerfectHeat technology for more efficient cooking with less fuel. An aluminized steel cooktop offers durable use and is rust resistant, and the removable grates and grease management tray offer simple and quick cleanup. The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ can run for up to one hour with both burners on high setting on one 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and produces 20,000 total BTUs of cooking power.
reviewText: I had high expectations for this grill. Yes, it did heat up things fast. The drawback is that the legs are only an inch tall. Which means if the table you are using is not a heat resistant surface,it will ruin your table. Second, there is no high cover you can purchase, if you want to grill something like,corn, or other meat that you want to cook longer, you cannot. Because the cover is about an inch high only. I just wish there is a cover I can buy for the grill side.

title: Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black
description: Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.
reviewText: Uneven heat and handle is too short.  Wish I had gotten something different.  I was really excited, as I had read so many positive reviews, and videos on how to convert this to LP from your already regulated rig line.  Sadly, after using this on multiple trips, I wish I had gotten something else.  It cooks very unevenly, and every-time I try to use the wheels, the lower table top pops out and drags/scrapes on the ground.  Just overall poorly designed and executed.  I've bought and used different swap tops for it, and it doesn't help.  All I've come up with is leaving one side on high to seer meats, and then placing them on the other side that is off (as low is still too hot) to slow cook.  Not what I wanted or was expecting.

title: Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set, Red
description:
Serve up your favorite foods around the campfire or portable stove with the Coleman 24-Piece Enamel Dinnerware Set. Made of double-fired enamel and featuring a timeless red speckled design, this outdoor dinnerware set offers classic style and durability and is dishwasher safe for easy cleaning. This 24-piece set includes four 10-inch dinner plates, four 12-ounce coffee mugs with handles, and four 6-inch bowls, making it a great choice for family meals at picnics, BBQs, and campsites. It also includes a silverware kit with four plastic-handled forks, spoons, and knives stored in a convenient roll-up pouch for easy organization and storage. Ideal for camping, picnicking, and even backyard use, this set of Coleman speckled dinnerware includes everything you need to eat a meal under the stars. The 24-piece set is outfitted with four 10-inch dinner plates, four 10-ounce mugs, and four 6-inch bowls, giving you and three co-campers enough containers for a main course, a side salad, and an after-dinner hot chocolate. In addition, the set comes with four knives, four spoons, and four forks, so you don't need to rely on plastic silverware when eating. Best of all, the set is durable and dishwasher-safe, with a red-speckled enamel surface that holds up to heavy use. The silverware also includes a compact carrying case to keep the cutlery bunched together. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: I always bought plastic utensils, Styrofoam cups and paper bowls and plates before getting this set.  It's perfect for a family of 4.  Everything in this set is durable and easy to clean.  The spoons are big and the knifes work great for butter and cutting steak.  It will definitely save me money over time!

title: Coleman 75-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels
description: Easily transport cold food and beverages with the Coleman 75-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. Perfect for sporting events and outdoor activities, this wheeled cooler comes equipped with 2 recessed, heavy-duty wheels that make transport easyeven when the cooler is filled to the brim. Simply extend and lock the telescoping handle, close the hinged lid, and wheel the cooler wherever you need to go. Premium insulation along the lid and walls helps ensure your items are kept cool, delivering ice retention of up to 5 days in temperatures as high as 90F. With enough capacity for up to 118 cans, the cooler provides ample space for barbecue ingredients, perishable food, drinks, and more. When it is time to go, the leak-resistant drain allows you to easily empty excess water without tilting the cooler. And thanks to its integrated cup holders, this wheeled cooler helps you keep a beverage always close at hand.
reviewText:
Love the size, the extendable pull handle is sturdy. The side carry handles (and front) look nothing like this one in the image on Amazon - the one I got has flimsy white slide-type handles that look and feel like they could snap at any time. See image posted.

Unfortunately, it doesn't do the "keep it cool" job nearly as well as our 15yr old Rubbermaid with a split-top lid and the same 4 cup holders (and way better, much sturdier side handles)... The lid on this Coleman feels thin (although it is sturdy enough for an adult to sit on top) and the whole lid has to open to retrieve something, it just wasn't keeping ice from melting as fast as our older cooler. We brought both to Lake Havasu recently, to compare them, with avg temps about 90-95*.

Just don't think this one will do the job or last nearly as long as the Rubbermaid - but it was worth a shot.

title: Coleman Outdoor Compact Table
description: The Coleman Compact Aluminum Table is the perfect camping and outdoor cooking companion. Hot pots and pans will not damage the aluminum slat top. Accordion style steel frame is sturdy and easy to set up.Table height 27.5".Table Weight 9 lbs. 8 oz..Folded Size: 5.5" x 5.5" x 27.5"..Weight capacity 100 lbs..Includes storage bag with carry strap.Directions conveniently sewn on to carry bag..Color: Silver.Import..
reviewText: Fairly light and compact when packed - and good height when set up. Not instant up, but not bad

title: Coleman Converta Cot
description: The Coleman Converta Cot provides comfortable seating at the campsite or in the backyard. Four back position and two foot positions for use as a lounge chair or cot.Unfolded Dimensions: 76 in. x 26 in. x 15.5 in..Folded Dimensions: 27.8 in. x 25 in. x 4.9 in..
reviewText: Love this chair for the first few weeks.....but then the back support broke......

title: Coleman Kids Wonder Lake 2-Person Dome Tent
description: Coleman 2000024383 tent kids wonder. Lake 2P dome, 4x7. This product is manufactured in Cambodia. Tents canopies hammocks.
reviewText: Lightweight and easy setup but zipper gets off track very easily. Bought a second to use and return the first but the second had the same issue.

title: Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent
description:
Get better airflow with the Coleman Rainfly Accessory for 6-Person Instant Tent. This rainfly for the Coleman 6-Person Instant Tent, 10 x 9, allows you to crack your windows during a downpour and still stay dry. Get better airflow when camping in your Coleman Instant six-person tent with this Instant tent rainfly. Coleman tents are well sealed and protected from the rain, so campers sometime lose airflow when it starts to pour. Designed for Coleman's 10-by-9-foot Instant tents, the rainfly not only provides added protection from the weather, but it also allows you to crack your windows during a storm and still stay dry. Plus, the rainfly creates a spacious vestibule outside--perfect for stowing shoes and other gear.

reviewText: The product description was not clear on what version of the 6 person 10x9 instant tent this would fit.  It does not fit my 10x9 and I have no idea how to try to return this.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: This case fits perfectly for the Triton 2 burner stove from Coleman. Excellent value and glad we purchased it. No, it does not have a hardback, that's okay by us.

title: Coleman Basic Propane 2-Burner Stove
description: Coleman 3000003653 stove pan 2 burner basic. Camping cooking stoves. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping cooking stoves
reviewText:
perfect love it,,, use it at our cabin
hooked up to 25lb tank

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
reviewText: We bought two of these water containers for camping.  If you need something to hold 5 gallons of water, this will do the job.  If the use of a spigot is important, I would not recommend this product.  The first problem is leaking.  The spigot valve itself actually shuts off fine. The problem is you cannot properly seal the connection between the cap and the canister.  The seam on at the mouth of the cannister leaves a ridge that the rubber seal cannot overcome no matter how tight the cap is twisted on the threads. The second problem is the handle on one of the spigots broke off on our first camping trip.

title: Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case
description: On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. With an ultra-portable design and included carry case, this stove is ready for any camping adventure and quickly sets up on any outdoor tabletop, supporting up to a 10-inch pan. Attach an 8.8 oz. butane cylinder (sold separately), light the automatic matchless ignition with the turn of a knob, and in no time, youre cooking with up to 7,650 BTUs of power for up to 1.25 hours. Wind baffles shield your flame from the wind, and a large stable base makes stirring easy. Use your stove for years to come thanks to an easy-to-clean, durable porcelain-coated grate, rust-proof aluminum burner, and made-to-last enameled steel exterior.
reviewText: love it, small and works great; I had similar one before; very satisfied

title: Guide Series Dual-Fuel Camping Stove
description: With fuel-efficient burners and a wind-blocking design, the Coleman Guide Series Dual-Fuel Stove gives you the ability to make family meals for any outdoor adventure. The two independently adjustable Band-a-Blu burners in this portable stove combine for 14,000 BTU of reliable cooking power. Dual Fuel technology conveniently uses either Coleman Liquid Fuel or unleaded gasoline (both sold separately) to efficiently create steady heat with less fuel. This propane stove has adjustable Wind Block panels that provide wind protection while giving you the stovetop space to use two 10-inch pans at the same time. All Season Strong technology helps this propane gas stove function well in all conditions, from summer tailgates to winter hunting trips. The durable chrome-plated grate on this camping stove is removable for easy cleaning.
reviewText: We have one and bought this as a gift for special friends at Christmas.  It was a big hit!  Can't go wrong.

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.
reviewText: I've owned two of these, and both died premature deaths. The first one cracked (okay, let's try and be a little more careful...but it wasn't abused), the second one had a spout that never did thread on right--it was as if it was missing a thread or two. So if you get the right one, I suppose it might suffice for basic, non-rugged duty water carrying, but inspect closely before paying(I guess that means you're out of luck if you buy online).

title: Coleman Sundome Dome Tent
reviewText: Easy to set up, slightly harder to compact to put back in its carrying case. Allows some moisture to come in and isn't very cold heat retentive. However it has a sturdy design and looks nice.

title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
reviewText:
Good size.
But it seemed to be damaged. The fill material did not seem enought. For me not comfortable at all. It semed the bag was not filled properly.

title: Coleman Camping First Aid Kit an All Purpose First Aid Kit for Emergencies at Home, Car, Workplace, Hiking, or Survival, 205-Pieces
description: The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the ultimate all purpose first aid kit for larger groups or outdoor adventures. This 205 piece kit features components needed in the field, including various standard and specialty bandages, first aid tape, gauze pads, antiseptic towels, antibiotic ointment, sting relief wipes, a cold pack and common first aid tools. All the first aid supplies are organized in a zipped, durable, semi-hard case. The unique EZ Find System keeps your items organized and easy to find during an emergency. There is space in the kit for your specialized medical supplies or medications so you can fully customize this first aid kit to fit your needs. The Coleman Expedition First Aid Kit is the perfect first aid kit for your home, RV, car, bug out bag, or for groups when camping, hiking, at sporting events, workplace, and more.
reviewText: great purchase

title: Coleman Compression Sack
description: Whether you're packing or storing your gear, reduce your bulk with a Coleman Compression Sack. Its unique design compresses large sleeping bags or other soft items with three straps that tighten around the outside. When you're ready to go, the sturdy web handle on the bottom makes one-handed carrying simple.
reviewText:
Would give 1 star if it wasn't so cheap. Fell apart with minimal use. Seams especially near the straps gave way. The straps themselves are double stitched but could use reinforcement on the excess bit. The stuff sack material itself is very thin which inherently makes it very light and perfect for backpacking. Maybe one or two trips and this will fall apart. Getting all the seams double stitched with thicker thread.

Will steer clear of this. Already ordered one from REI which is about the same in cost but hopefully better in quality.

title: Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole, 8-Foot
description: Bring new life to an old tent or canopy with the Coleman Steel Replacement Tent Pole. Compatible with most camping tents and canopies, this durable tent pole easily adjusts from 3 to 8 feet to offer sturdy support where needed. A friction lock secures the replacement pole at the desired height and ensures the pole will not budge when holding up a canopy, tent, or other shelter. Durable steel construction gives this pole the reliable strength to withstand the rigors of outdoor use, helping bring older tents back to their full potential.
reviewText: I would have given it a 4 but had to fix everyone of them . After I fixed they work great, and are very strong.

title: Coleman Butane / Propane Mix Fuel (Mix Fuel 7.75 oz.) - 7.75 oz.
description: Butane/propane mix canisters are lightweight, resalable, and easily connect to stoves and lanterns. Most canister appliances are lightweight and simple to use, so if you are a backpacker who counts ounces and appreciates convenience this fuel is for you. Downsides are that canisters can't be recycled, and in subfreezing temperatures, the fuel does not perform well. Or at all. Cold temperatures affect the pressure in the canister, so performance is best in mild to moderate conditions, main advantages: convenience and light weight includes a collar notch on top of the canister that provides easy alignment in appliances
reviewText: I rated the fuel canister three stars because it's heavy and large. I placed it inside my cook kit,fits nicely. For those on long backpacking treks(i.e. Appalachian Trail)they may want this size,but not for the minimalist. It takes too much space in my daypack and bug out bag and 75 cubic inch pack.,too much weight as well. Best left for car camping for a back up to the Coleman two or one burner propane stove.

title: Coleman Collapsible Chest Cooler (Color May Vary)
description: The Coleman Collapsible Chest Cooler offers the space and reliability of a hard-sided cooler, with the convenience of a collapsible design. The Collapsible Chest holds 72 twelve-ounce cans, with durable side walls for rigidity when the cooler is in use. Dual front pockets offer extra storage for utensils and more, while easy-access ports on top let you grab just what you need from the inside. When not in use, the Chest collapses completely flat for convenient storage and packing.
reviewText: Better than expected!!

title: Coleman Cast Iron Skillet
description: Grill up burgers, steaks and chicken on your next outdoor adventure with a Coleman Cast Iron Skillet. You'll have plenty of room to cook on the easy-to-clean, non-stick, 10-in,-diameter cooking surface. Raised ribs ensure an even temperature throughout, and the pour spouts makes for easy draining when you're done. A hole in the molded handle makes storing at home or at the campsite simple.
reviewText: Over priced considering you have to order regulator and hose separately. Cheaply made in China, instructions very poor. The burner holes were mostly plugged with paint and had to use a drill bit to clean out. Flame adjustment not the greatest, if you turn it down anywhere below high the flame goes out. Will keep it as it will work for what I use it for, sand shading veneer for inlay projects.

title: Coleman Packable SUV Quickbed
description: The Coleman SUV Quickbed is sized just right to fit in the back of most SUVs (including Tahoe, Suburban, Xterra, Xplorer, Trailblazer and more) -- and it's equally convenient in your tent or guest room! The SUV Quickbed measures 70" long by 41" wide when fully inflated -- or adjust it down to 63" long to fit your SUV. Coleman's ComfortSmart coil system features 45 coils for a firm, reliable sleep; the Quickbed is topped with soft suede for luxurious comfort. It's durably constructed of heavy-duty, puncture-resistant PVC. The Quickbed is easy to inflate and deflate, thanks to Coleman's extra-wide Advanced Double Lock Valve. Like all Coleman inflatable products, this Quickbed features Coleman's Airtight System, guaranteed not to leak. And the built-in Wrap 'N' Roll storage system makes packing and storing quick and easy.
reviewText: When this item was smaller than I expected, Coleman declined to let me return it. I guess it was what I deserved by going by the size of our SUV instead of reading the specs, but in this world of mail order, easy returns are important if the whole system is going to work. This was the second coleman product I have had a problem with, and I wonder if their quality is falling off.

title: Coleman Beach Shade
description: " Take your own shady, private space to the beach or park with the Coleman Beach Shade. This pop-up beach tent is made with UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays and durable Polyguard material that is designed to last season after season. The extended floor can be zipped shut for privacy while changing clothes, and the dry line offers a convenient place to hang wet bathing suits and towels. Integrated storage pockets give you a secure place to put your items while you swim or sunbathe. And when it gets hot, the back window can be unzipped to let the breeze in, while the sand bag compartments can be filled as needed to anchor the beach canopy on windy days. The Coleman Beach Shade comes with ground stakes and pre-attached guy lines and can be set up in about five minutes.
reviewText:
1)  After taking it all of the way to Florida for our vacation, upon opening the product one of the black tent poles was defective.  One of the two black poles was missing a "metal ferrule" in the middle of the pole assemble, meaning the pole did not lock together.  I guess i should have checked before taking it all of the way to Florida, but I did not because you know - it always takes up a bit more space after re-packing.
2) After going through the warranty process, Coleman sent a "Tent Pole Replacement Kit".  This "kit" was several other fiberglass poles, an elastic cord, end tips, and ferrules.  SOME ASSEMBLY REQUIRED and I had to make my own replacement pole with the kit.  I had to cut the poles to the correct size (with a hacksaw), thread the elastic cord, and add the end caps myself.
3) The replacement poles are thicker than the original, and as a result the assembled pole is more rigid than the other black pole, meaning the assembled shade is now lopsided.
4)  The four replacement pole segments, when folded for storage, are too long to be stored in the carrying case. Even though the total length of the pole when assembled is the correct length.  Originally there were five  pole segments and the replacement kit has four.
NOT happy

title: Coleman Cooler Tie-Down Kit
description: Everything you need to secure your cooler in your boat, RV or other vehicle. The Coleman Cooler Mounting Kit fits all Coleman chest coolers and other standard chest coolers. The kit includes 4 sturdy corner brackets, 2 fully-adjustable tie-downs with strong stainless-steel hooks, and 12 screws.
reviewText: Broke after a month.

title: Coleman 12-In. Steel Tent Stakes
description: You'll sleep soundly knowing your tent is secured with Coleman 12-In. Steel Tent Stakes. These heavy-duty stakes are specially-designed to break through some of the toughest ground.
reviewText: Good quality for the price.

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue

reviewText: We LOVED this water carrier! We bought 3 for a camping weekend recently, and couldn't be more pleased. The pressure release valve was awesome, and the spout was great as well. I highly recommend these for emergency water storage or camping.

reviewText: Like the hard case and the extra room to add what you want and need.

title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
reviewText: This is the second cooler of this model that broke on us mid-trip. Bought in May and used it only 2 weekends (I'm in August as I write). I would think Coleman could make coolers that last longer than a few months. Highly dissatisfied. Get a better cooler.

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue

reviewText: Works well and durable.

title: Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Aluminum Grill Griddle, Full Size
description: Get more meals out of your Coleman RoadTrip Grill by adding a durable Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Full Size Aluminum Griddle. Swaptop compatible, you can mix and match it with other cook tops (sold separately) for more meal options. It creates 285 sq. in. of cooking space for pancakes, bacon and more.
reviewText: It works quite well, but not being able to close the grill is a definite thumbs down.  As long as you don't mind,  it is worth the money.

title: Coleman 48-Quart Performance Cooler
description:
Keep your food and drinks cold, and keep the fun going, for up to 3 days when you bring a Coleman 48 Quart Performance Cooler. Large enough to hold 63 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the 2-way handles make getting to your destination easy. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant drain makes it easy to empty any excess water, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. The Coleman 48-Quart Chest Cooler combines the easy-to-carry convenience of two-way handles with the rugged durability of a hard plastic exterior and hinged lid, making it the perfect cooler for afternoons on the beach or on the game field. This innovative cooler is constructed with Thermozone insulation to keep food and beverages fresh and cold. This American-made cooler also features a rust-proof, leak-proof drain for easy emptying and cleaning, and it has the capacity to hold up to 63 12-ounce cans. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: Keeps things cold for a decent amount of time.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: I ordered the proper case per model number but it fit so tight I tore the handles off putting the stove in it. wish I didn't waste the money.

title: Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory
description: The Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory lets you enjoy added protection from bad weather while increasing airflow inside your tent. Coleman tents are sealed strongly to prevent water from entering, which means campers can sometimes lose airflow when it starts to rain and they zip up the windows. Thats why this rainfly accessory is designed to fit over the top of 8- by 7-foot Coleman 4-Person Instant Tents (sold separately), providing added rain protection while giving you the ability to open windows without having to worry about rainwater getting inside.
reviewText: Perfect! once I ordered the right size!

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: Sundome 2P Dome TentFeatures:Up to 33% more water resistant verses a comparable standard Coleman tent Weathertec System patented welded floors and inverted seams help keep water outSnag-Free continuous pole sleeves for easy setup Ground Vent for increased ventilationTwo windows for great ventilation and viewsDoor awning helps keep the rain out E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside your tent Storage pocket to keep your gear organized Expandable carry bag lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packingSpecifications:Doors:1 D-DoorRainfly:PolyguardTent:Polyguard,Polyester Mesh 68DPoles:FiberglassWeight: 7.2 (3.3 KG)<br/
reviewText: This tent was used for a weekend away at a motorcycle rally that my husband went to.  Of course hotel rooms were booked as he decided to go last minute so he got a tent site at a nearby campground.  The tent is compact enough in it's case/carry bag to be able to fit on top of the side bag of his Harley.  It was easily removed from it's carry bag, easy to put up and definitely roomy enough for two.  The bathtub like bottom helped to keep the moisture away from his sleeping bag and other personal items.  With the vents on the tent it was a little chilly (it actually went down to 45 degrees at night in Texas) so this needs to be kept in mind when deciding what you are using it for and where.  It was easy to take down and put it back into it's carry case.  Overall a great tent for the price and good quality!

title: Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern
description: The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.
reviewText: For sturdy, this in no way competes with the Coleman lanterns of my youth--my earliest memory in the mid-1950s ran on a can of fuel and lit the night when needed in a farm setting, a real tool. The sturdiest part is the metal D-ring handle on the bottom that one uses to engage the "use batteries" or "saves batteries" feature. It is all plastic, and not very impressive plastic, at that--the 4 D batteries are as heavy as the entire housing.  However, this lightness contributes to its ability to purportedly survive a drop on a hard surface up to one meter. Overall, the size is similar to a two liter bottle, minus the screw top. The best thing going for it is the ability to protect the battery life, when not in use and the choice of light output to make the burning lamp last as long as possible. One will have to decide if the investment for one emergency light source is worth the going price on the day one shops.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText:
It is ok but to me not a axe ok but to thick to be a great hatchet ,,I'm 60 years old and do know the difference ok it's about 10 inches long with a nail puller .my grandson wanted hatchet so we got him this it came in very thick on edge and wouldn't cut butter ,,did ok only after I fueled on it then I took sand paper and got it a
Title sharp after 2 hours of hard work then with a fine whit stone so took half a day my grand baby went to Home Depot and bought a frisk era brand hatchet and was happy but no mail pouch now want fit in the one he bought from Coleman ,,but it was cheep so I'm putting with my tools. .im sure I can find a use for it

title: Coleman 2000016402 Mess Kit, Aluminum, 5-Pc.
description: 5 Piece, Aluminum Mess Kit, Includes 8 OZ Cup, 7" Fry Pan, 16 OZ Pot With Lid & 6.5" Dish Pan, Pieces Nest Inside Each Other & Is Covered With A Lid For Easy Storage & Transport.
reviewText: Dented...  Brand new right out of the box.

title: Coleman 70 Quart Xtreme 5 Cooler
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time, and make the fun last longer, when you bring a Coleman 70 Quart Xtreme 5 Cooler. Xtreme technology uses an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls to keep your items cold for up to 5 days. Large enough to hold 98 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on a long weekend camping trip, a roadtrip, or out to a BBQ, the 2-way handles make getting to your destination easy. Once you arrive, the cup holders molded into the lid will keep your cold drink close while you enjoy the day. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. The Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Cooler is the ideal traveling companion. The large icy blue cooler and white lid are lined with a full two full inches of insulation and the hinged lid also features a secure and tight gasket seal, ensuring that your cooler will keep food and beverages chilled for up to five days at temperatures of up to 90 degrees. Holding up to 98 beverage cans with ice, this cooler features two-way handles for easy lift and maneuvering in tight spaces, and the sump-lined drain promises easy clean-up and no-tilt draining--a plus for this large capacity cooler. This models lid can be fitted with 24-inch cords (not included) for additional dry storage--lawn chairs, beach towels, picnic blankets, etc.--on top. The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText:
It's a typical coleman cooler.  Plastic and and works good.  I've owned a few and they work great.  I put two bags of ice and lots of drinks in the cooler the day we got it.  5 days later there was still some ice.  Have not had it out camping yet and that will be the real test.  I'll update then.

UPDATE:  Second campout and it leaks and the handel came off.  JUNK!  JUNK!  JUNK!

Not worth $10 in my opinion.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText: It's a nice large tent, but I wish it had better ventilation and more storage pockets. Easy to set up and take down. Held up well in moderate winds.

title: Coleman BlackCat PerfecTemp Catalytic Heater with InstaStart Technology
description:
A great portable heater to take with you on a camping trip, to a sports outing or in your golf cart, the Coleman BlackCat InstaStart Heater features a 3000-BTU heater that will run for up to 7 hours on a single 16.4-ounce can of propane (sold separately). The BlackCat features PerfecTemp Catalytic technology, for safe, efficient, flameless heat, while Coleman's InstaStart battery ignition provides push-button, matchless lighting. A convenient switch offers both High and Low temperature settings for more control. The BlackCat Heater's legs fold up for easy storage, and an easy-grip handle provides comfortable, convenient carrying. Limited 5-year warranty. The Coleman 5038-850 BlackCat Perfectemp Catalytic Propane Heater with InstaStart Technology provides you with comfortable warmth indoors or in a tent. This catalytic heater has an electronic ignition which is not only quicker and easier that using a match, but is much more reliable in cold, windy weather. This Coleman BlackCat heater is incredibly portable for its heat output, with an easy-to-grip handle that makes it easy to carry, and compact legs that integrate into its collar for easy storage. The BlackCat's 3,000 BTU output will operate for up to seven hours on one 16.4 ounce cylinder of propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText:
Upsides: It works.

Downsides: The InstaStart thingy doesn't. No matter how many times I press the button, I see the little spark but apparently not enough propane is in the vicinity of that spark to fire it off. I use a long-snout butane barbecue lighter to light the thing without being barbecued.

It's not very adjustable. There's low heat. There's high heat. That's it.

The metal grill on the front gets very hot. You must be very careful where you place this thing so that said grill doesn't contact you, your tent, your dog, your upholstery in the small van you're trying to heat up, or anything else flammable. You'd think they could at least put some sort of flared guard or bumpers around the front to help keep stuff from coming in contact with that metal grill, sort of like the "bumpers" on chicken house heaters to keep their metal grill from contacting stuff if the heater is knocked over, but apparently that'd require too much thought.

There's only one safe way to place this heater -- on its side with the legs extended, as shown in the product photo. You can't stand it up like a candle, it might fall over and then that metal ring around the front (which gets very hot) would contact something that you probably don't want contacted.

This doesn't put out much heat on high heat but still drains a 1 pound can of propane within four hours. No problem, you say, you'll just hook it up to the 20 pound tank for your barbecue and have plenty of heat for all night camping for a week? Uhm, no. a) there is no way to hook a 20 pound tank to this heater, and b) if you did rig up a hose of some sort, see the part about only one safe way to place this heater -- by removing the rear "leg" (the 1 pound canister), the heater is now in an attitude that is unsafe. Coleman says "don't do it" and they're right -- this heater simply is designed wrong to be safe when used with an external propane tank.

There's no safety cut-off to turn off the heater if it gets bumped and falls over from the pictured attitude, which as I noted is the only safe attitude. So don't leave the heater alone with a dog  or large cat that might bump into it and make it fall over, or you might come back to a smoldering tent and barbecued Fido/Fluffy.

All in all, this heater works, but it's profoundly mediocre. I've used it in my garage to supplement an electric heater that struggles with cooler temperatures, but due to its limitations never used it for camping as originally intended, and probably won't ever do so. In particular, the very hot grill on the front is such a safety hazard that I refuse to use it anywhere but on the concrete floor of my garage, where if it falls over all that'll happen is that it contacts non-flammable concrete.

I'm now researching better options for a tent heater, because this just isn't acceptable to me. You'd have to haul along a metal roasting pan and a piece of fiberglass insulation to make this safe to use in a tent (the pan to hold the heater, the fiberglass insulator to go under the pan to insulate it from the tent floor), and at that point you're into a realm of clunkiness that makes me say, "eh, time to do something else."

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
I received this tent about a week ago knowing full well it would be used right away. Though it was an eight person tent, I was the only one sleeping in it but I needed the extra room for my cot and all of my gear. The time of year is mid summer so I thought this tent would be perfect.
When the tent came, I opened the item right away to take inventory and to do a test assembly in the field across from my home. Please understand I do not do have a lot of current camping experience but I have had experience over the years and thought it should be pretty straight forward. The tent includes the tent, rainfly, stakes and poles all enclosed with instructions in a zippered bag. The tent instructions say that it would be better if a second person was available to help erect the tent. There was no second person included so I did it solo. It takes some patience but it can be done. Don't get ahead of yourself, follow the steps and complete them one at a time and you'll have success even if you are solo. The hardest part is the initial raising and placing of the main support poles. If you first secure the main poles (one at a time) on the side opposite the door, you'll find it a bit easier as you can stand inside the door (if it is zipped open,) allowing you to be closer to the center of the roof as it is raised. After that, everything is a bit easier. Side poles come next and then clip and secure everything.
 You may find that the zippers on the front door are under a bit of tension. I found that if I used the top zipper to close half the door and the lower zipper to close the lower half, it opened and closed much easier than if you used one zipper to do the whole door. Also, you'll find if you leave the internal door window unzipped (open), the door zippers work easier. One of the back window zippers kept loosing it's lanyard as the metal tang on the zipper wedge seemed a bit too open to keep the cord in place so I simple took my uni-tool pliers and gave it a gentle squeeze closing the loop and thus holding the lanyard more securely. No failure on the zippers thus far.
 The floor is a Tyvec type material and a tub type design. It seemed fairly rugged but I stilled used a tarp ground cloth as a good foundation. My cot has 6 legs and in four days never left as much as even an impression on the floor. I did have a thorn or two pierce the floor while camping, my fault for not making sure the ground was completely clear. But it did serve as a reminder that even though it may look rugged, you can still easily put a hole in it.
 On my camping trip, I used what I had learned to erect the tent and repeated the process with out too much difficulty even on my own. It is important to note at this point that I did not have success putting on the rainfly during my practice run as the wind was too great. So, if you can, save the rain fly for when there is little or no wind, especially if you are by yourself. I slept in the tent for three days without the rain fly and it was great. The huge screen panels allow great views of the night sky and give more than ample ventilation.
 The forecast for the fourth night was for rain so I installed the rainfly. This is where someone may have dropped the ball in writing the instructions. First, the instructions call for placing the Arbor  rods through the specified points prior to draping the fly over the tent. If you have two people, perhaps its possible. But, it was much easier to drape the fly over the tent and THEN install the arbor rods. Second, the instructions show four bungee securing loops, one at each corner of the fly when in fact there are 8 bungee loops with hooks. The hooks are then stretched and secured to the stake loops at the four main corners of the tent. This design could use a little refining as the steel tent stake anchor loops are pressed firmly to the ground under pressure from both the tent stakes themselves and the four tent support rods. Trying to get the bungee hook attached to the tent stake loop as directed may require you to do a little excavation with your fingernails in order to access enough surface area of the loop to secure the hook. Also, these bungees are under pressure so when you are stretching them, be careful one doesnt slip and hit you right in the face...like it did me.
 Once the fly was up, I was ready for the rain. Or was I? In total, we had about an inch and a half of rain that fell over a 24 hour period. In that time, you could see a slow deterioration of the rain proof capability in both the rain fly and the tent. At first it looked like it was coming in around the tent stake anchor points (it felt wet in these areas), then I noticed that the fly itself started to seep through the fabric in several areas. Later still, I noticed that the tent fabric areas that were not protected/covered by the rain fly became saturated and accumulating moisture seeped through. I used several t shirts and socks to soak up the water. In total, I estimate there was at least a quart or more that leaked through. Fortunately, the ground cloth raised the floor of the tent slightly and accumulating water stayed to the outer perimeter of the floor. Considering it was a new tent, I am pretty disappointed at the lack of water proofing that was done to both the tent and the rain fly. I expected some moisture, but not to the degree that I experienced.
 As far as rain is concerned, there is a another design feature that could be improved and that is the rain fly arbor over the front door. With out any wind, rain can still wet the door. And, since the door is at an angle, when you unzip the door, the door swings inside and all of the rain on the outside of the door is now on your floor. You can minimize this by tapping on the door before you open it and most of the rain water will fall to the outside, but not all of it. An over hang of another 12-18 inches would take care of this.
 One final note. The tent take down was pretty simple all but drying it out before I could roll everything up which takes time. Try as I may, I could not roll up everything tight enough to be able to fit everything back into the bag that it came in. A bigger bag might be nice. I have since purchased a can of water proofing spray and will treat everything and give it another go. If I hadn't received such a huge discount when I purchased it, I would  probably return the tent because of the leaking.
