title: Coleman Camping Coffee Maker
description: Easily make drip-brewed coffee while enjoying the outdoors with the Coleman Camping Coffee Maker. Designed with a steel base that fits over most 2- and 3-burner camp stoves, this camping coffee maker features a large reservoir that enables you to brew up to 10 cups of hot coffee at once. Making coffee with this outdoor coffee maker is easy. Simply spoon grounds into the removable swing-out filter basket, fill the reservoir with water, and place the coffee maker over your stove burner. Within minutes, the 10-cup glass carafe will be filled with freshly brewed coffee ready to be enjoyed in the great outdoors. A Pause 'N Serve feature lets you grab a cup before the brew cycle is finished. The Coleman Camping Coffee Maker measures 8.5 x 11 x 14 inches. About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. END
reviewText:
We purchased one of these at WALMART before a camping trip. I have a 2 burner Ozark camp stove that has a wide grill over the burners. In order to get the coffee maker onto the fire, I had to remove the grill from the stove and set the coffee maker right on the frame over the burner. With the burner on high, even the slightest breeze stopped the coffee maker from getting enough heat to make coffee. It took over 1/2 hour to get a pot of coffee. Inside a camper or in the house over a gas stove I think it would work better. Outside a tent, with a cold breeze, this unit is USELESS. My first cup of coffee was made in my canteen cup over a can of gel camp fuel.
 I'm not totally disappointed in the coffee maker as it did eventually make a pot of coffee. I'll keep it as a back up for the house should we loose power.

title: Coleman Calgary 20 Degree Sleeping Bag
description: The Coleman Calgary Scoop Sleeping Bag is great for cold-weather camping. It measures 33-Inch wide by 78-Inch long, and fits most people up to 5-Inch11-Inch. The unique scoop shape makes a comfortable headrest, keeping your head and pillow off the ground. The Calgary Sleeping Bag is durably constructed of a soft brushed-polyester cover and liner, with 4 pounds of ColeTherm insulation to keep you comfortable down to 40-20Degree Fahrenheit. The Calgary features the QuickCord storage system for easy storage -- no tying required. Coleman-Inchs ComfortSmart technology means your sleeping bag is packed with features to keep you warm and comfortable: ZipPlow plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags; Comfort Cuff surrounds your face with softness; FiberLock prevents insulation from shifting keeping you warm and increasing durability; ThermoLock reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer; Zipper Glide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around corners; and Roll Control locks the bag in place for easier rolling.
reviewText: The fabric is not comfortable and is not what I thought it was.  Like laying in a soft rain jacket.

title: Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler
description: The Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler is perfect for weekend getaways, with the technology to keep ice up to five days in 90-degree weather and the capacity to hold 82 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Durable plastic construction. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 82 cans. Includes hinged lid with 4 cup holders and 2-way handle. Dimensions: 27.75L x 14.95W x 17.25H in..
reviewText:
I just got back from a six day camping trip. I filled this cooler  with 20 pounds of ice cubes (leaving about 1/3rd of the space free for food) and much to my surprise, the ice was gone early today but the water was icy cold still and the remaining food was fine.

 In other words, this cooler performs phenomenally if the ratio of ice to food is what is needed.

 I did keep it in the shade, but the temperatures were in the 80s, and I am very very happy with it's excellent performance.

title: Coleman 0°F Mummy Sleeping Bag for Big and Tall Adults | North Rim Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag
reviewText: I am 6' and 215. This bag is way too small for me. It's warm, but way to small, you can't even turn in it.

title: Coleman 2000014864 Mosquito Head Net
description: Enjoy the outdoors without the pests when you're wearing a Coleman Mosquito Head Net. The fine mesh stops insects from biting, while still allowing you to see nature's beauty. The Mosquito Head Net is designed especially for wide, all-around brim hats, but still fits other styles well. Keep flying insects out when hiking or working in mosquito- or gnat-ridden territory with the Coleman insect head net. The net is designed to fit comfortably over most headgear, and is made with a 1mm maximum netting grade.
reviewText: seem good

title: NFL Soft-Sided Insulated Cooler Bag, 9-Can Capacity with Ice (All Team Options)
reviewText: it seems like very good quality but its too small to be used like a lunch box

title: Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad
description: Get a good night's rest with the Coleman Rest Easy Camp Pad. The closed cell foam protects you from uncomfortable surfaces and helps insulate you from the cold ground.
reviewText:
If this pad had been a few inches longer it would have gotten 5 stars from me.

It's not super cushy, so you need a flat and smooth place to lay it on, but it does provide some cushioning against the hard ground.  We got this to use as an isolation mat, not for its cushioning, and it does that job exceptionally well.  It's wide enough to easily fit a "thick" adult on it, from shin to head (I am 5'10") and keep the ground from sucking all the heat out of you.  The bottom side of me was the warmest side of me while laying on this mat, and that was in 35* nights, so I would guess that even at cooler temperatures it would do a good job of insulating your body from the cold ground.
For the price, it can't be beat for its insulating properties, but if you are looking for a "feather bed" affect, this isn't the mat for you.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: Perfect size for keeping in camping supplies. Will need sharpening, comes in with a very slight edge.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: This is great for my stove that doesn't have a very good handle and of course there are pockets for tanks on either end.  The stove fits pretty tight.  They could have made the dimensions of the carrying case a little more generous so that you are not stretching the zipper to close the case.

title: Coleman Steel-Belted Portable Cooler, 54 Quart
description: Go back in time with cold food and drinks today when you take a Coleman 54 Quart Steel Belted Cooler along with you. It looks just like the one you remember with its classic design first developed in 1954. Large enough to hold 85 cans and tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on an overnight camping trip, to a tailgate party, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip steel handles and stainless steel lid latch make getting to your destination easy, comfortable and secure. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax while you enjoy the day with a cold drink in hand. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. A Coleman Steel Belted Cooler will rekindle your fondest outdoor memories  and equip you to make many new ones.
reviewText: Product came damaged, had to return. Not willing to try again.

title: Coleman 40-Can Collapsible Soft Cooler
description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't. The Coleman 40 Can Collapsible Cooler is big enough to hold almost two cases of soda and then collapse down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. A bungee cord on the back, front pocket and external lid storage make keeping snacks and utensils convenient. An adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses. Heat-welded seams help prevent soft liner leaks.
reviewText: PURCHASED BECAUSE IT WAS COLLAPSIBLE AND COULD USE ON MY MOTORCYCLE, BUT THE BOTTOM HARDER SECTION GOT A SMALL HOLE IN IT AFTER USING 2 TIMES ,AND NOW WHEN THE ICE MELTS IS FILLS UP TO BOTTOM SECTION. OTHER THAN THAT IT WORKS WELL .

title: Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower
description: Don't miss a shower  even when you're camping  with the Coleman 5-Gallon Solar Shower. The reservoir holds up to 5 gallons of waterenough for multiple showers. It's constructed of heat-locking material that warms the water when it's left in the sun, even on cool days. The shower features a strong handle for reliable hanging and carrying as well as a handy on/off shower head valve to conserve water.
reviewText: I was initially concerned about how well the PVC would hold up but I've had this shoved into the storage area of my Jeep CJ-7 with loads of metal for tops and chairs and fishing gear. The shower has yet to be punctured or really even dis-colored. I'm pleasantly surprised. I go to the beach frequently in the summer and I was looking for a way to get the sand off the kids before I get them back in the Jeep. This fits the bill and the temperature is better than the showers that are provided.

title: Coleman NEW Outdoor Camping Waterproof 6 Person Instant Tent - 10'x9' Foootprint
description: The Coleman 6 Person Camping Instant Tent comes with the WeatherTec system that guarantees to keep you dry. This tent has poles that are pre attached to the tent, and takes only one minute to set up and take down. It features a spacious interior, 150D fabric (twice as thick as regular tent fabric) and there's no need for a separate rainfly because the tent is fully taped. The tent can accommodate two queen sized beds, and with a center height of 6 feet, there's plenty of room to move around and get situated. Great for a weekend trip with the whole family.
reviewText: Easy to set up and take down.  This tent was great, very roomy for 5 of us.  However, during camping, we experienced a heavy rainstorm, and our tent started to leak from the top middle part.  Next time we'll just place a small tarp over the top.  Other than the leak, it's a good tent.

title: Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case
description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough, double-walled case is made of polyethylene housing. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool and place your lantern inside the case, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
reviewText: I have a Coleman 5155 and this case fits the lantern perfectly.  It protects the glass globe when it's in the bed of the truck with the rest of the camping gear.

reviewText: Do not buy this pad! I agree with all the bad reviews found here. You may as well sleep on the ground with no pad...that is how useless this Coleman pad is. Do yourself a favor and go buy a hammock tent and never sleep on the ground again.

title: Coleman Company Men's Stearns Infinity Series Antimicrobial Personal Floatation Device
description: Enjoy a day at the lake or on the beach in the comfort of a Stearns Men's Infinity Series Antimicrobial Life Jacket. You'll feel great for hours when you buckle into the four adjustable straps on this US Coast Guard-approved PFD. With antimicrobial protection that resists odours, a durable 200D nylon shell and PE foam inside, you'll be taking on wakes and waves in it season after season.
reviewText: Very small. Not true to size at all

title: Coleman Aluminum Non-stick Griddle
description: Cook up bacon, eggs and pancakes on your next outdoor adventure with a durable, lightweight Coleman Aluminum Non-stick Griddle. You'll have plenty of room to create on a cooking surface large enough to fit two and three-burner stoves. When you're done, that cooking space cleans up quickly and easily.
reviewText: everything stuck to it. Bought for making pancakes. didn't do the job

title: Coleman Men's 35mm PVC/Poly Rain Suit
description: Keep yourself reliably dry and protected from the elements with the Coleman Men-Inchs PVC/Poly Rain Suit. Constructed of durable, waterproof 35mm PVC-coated polyester, the Rain Suit includes a rain jacket and pants. The jacket features a drawstring hood, center front zipper with storm flap to keep out moisture, back and armhole vents for air flow, and two lower pockets. The pants feature a drawstring elasticized waist for a good fit, leg openings with zipper closure & snap adjustment, articulated knees for ease of movement, and 2 convenient slit pockets that give you access to pockets on the pants you wear underneath. The Rain Suit stores compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText: UPDATE... I wore this on a 2 mile walk in a light rain. Though the suit is still new looking and has no rips or tears, I was wet down to my underwear. Not soaked mind you but damp enough that the cell phone in my pocket got a foggy screen and is now sitting in a bucket  of rice. I initially reviewed this on looks alone as I ordered it before the rainy season. I assumed that, because my 20 year old Coleman suit (from when they were still made in New Braunfels TX USA) looked very similar. But my old one is practically shredded and it still offers better protection than this sub standard Chinese suit. It is better than nothing but not by much. save some money and cut a hole in a trash bag. It will work better.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: Average expected better quality from Coleman. The axe head IS NOT hardened steel, and will dent.

title: Coleman Lantern Hanger
description: Whether it's fuel or battery-powered, the Coleman Lantern Hanger can hold your bail-handled camp light. The chain wraps tightly around trees, limbs and posts, and the support pegs give extra stability when you set the lantern on the hook. The hanger is easy to use and safe for trees.
reviewText: It's a great concept, and can work. But I just could not get it to hold my dual fuel lantern. First problem is the angle it is held at. The little "dip" that holds the lantern handle is held at a low point that I do not trust holding a $50 flammable object. The second problem is the S-hook that you connect the chain with is constantly falling off. I have lost mine after one weekend. I just got a nice nail from my toolbox, hammered it in, and hung my lantern. I'll play with this a little more and see if it can meet my needs.

title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
reviewText: This did not fit my stove - I sent it back!

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: For the money, this thing is quality!!  I put a cooler shock dry shell pack with a towel (because it got too cold!) and my granddaughter's Calciworms in it to keep them at 50-60 degrees.  (they're for her bearded dragon)  It's a neat, inexpensive way to keep them for a month or more and saves me money. I'm really pleased...The worm company recommened a wine cooler!!! I think those are way more expensive!!!

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
***Update***
DO NOT BUY THIS OR ANY COLEMAN TENT
We have only used this tent four times, it has been stored properly and never put up wet. The hooks that attach the tent to the poles on one half of the tent have all started ripping off. The other half is completely fine. I contacted Coleman about a possible quality control issue, and they were not only NO help, but also pretty rude about it. I expected to get more than four uses out of a product, and I certainly expected them to at least seem concerned about a possible quality control issue for their products. Shame on them!! I will be researching other brands for my family's camping needs.

We took this tent camping for the first time last Spring, and it worked very well.  It was much larger than what my husband was used to using, but I really liked it.  My son slept in one of the side compartments and we kept our gear in the other one.  In the middle, we had a queen sized air mattress.  We still had plenty of room to move around.  My husband also liked that he could stand up in the middle section of the tent.
It was very simple to set up, two of us set it up in about 20 minutes.  I think one person could set it up on their own, it would just take longer.  We had to break it down in the pouring rain, and even that was pretty easy to do.  Once we got it home and dried it out, everything easily went back into the bag it came in!
I really loved all of the little storage compartments in it.  We took our three year old son, and he loved being able to put his toys in them.
The only issue we had was with a small leak.  The last night we were camping, a really big storm blew through and it was absolutely pouring down rain.  One of the places where the pole attaches to the top of the tent started to drip into the tent.  In a lighter rain, we might not have even noticed it.  And my husband thinks he can weatherproof that spot to prevent us from having the problem in the future. But I should also mention that the bottom of the tent was literally floating on the rainwater in some places, and it did not leak one tiny bit!
All in all, we were very pleased with the tent!  You might just want to waterproof the spots where the tent poles attach to the tent before you take it out--just to be on the safe side.  :0)

title: Coleman PowerChill 3000001497 Thermoelectric Cooler with Power Supply (40-Quart), Silver
description:
Perfect for the office, dorm, RV, truck or car, the Coleman PowerChill Thermoelectric Cooler is fully portable, plugging into your vehicle's cigarette lighter. The PowerChill's new motor design provides quiet, long-lasting performance, cooling the contents down to 40-degreeF BELOW the surrounding temperature. The PowerChill is designed with many convenient features, including a patented, adjustable door that can open on the right or left side. The cooler can be used in two positions, either as an upright or a chest cooler. An adjustable shelf divider helps keep things organized inside. The PowerChill Cooler's molded handles make it easy to lift and carry. With a 40-quart interior that holds up to 44 cans of soda or beer in addition to snacks, the Coleman portable PowerChill thermoelectric cooler is ideal for the office, dorm, RV, truck, or car. The cooler is compatible with any vehicle's cigarette lighter. As a result, you can bring the PowerChill pretty much anywhere, from the campsite to the tailgater to your backyard BBQ. The PowerChill is equipped with a redesigned motor that provides quiet, long-lasting performance. The motor cools contents down to 40 degrees F below the surrounding temperature, so you don't have to fret once the ice starts melting in your standard cooler. The PowerChill includes several handy features, including a patented adjustable door that can open on both the right and left side, an adjustable shelf divider to keep things organized, and molded handles that make the cooler easy to lift and carry. The PowerChill even functions in two positions: upright or chest style. The PowerChill measures 15 by 21.75 by 17.125 inches (W x H x D). About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText:
I first used this cooler in my home as a temporary portable refrigerator.  It worked pretty well for that.  Sodas had just a slight amount of slush in them, which some may find too cold, but I thought it was perfect.  It says it will cool things to a temperature 40 degrees lower than the ambient air temperature and I guess that's why it cooled so well in my house.

I used it another time at a yard sale to keep sodas and water cold, but it took a really long time to cool the stuff down, much longer than a traditional cooler with ice.  By the time the stuff got cold enough to enjoy, the sale was over and we didn't need it any longer.

I also took it camping at an RV park and again, the results were lackluster at best.  It was a 3 hour drive to the campsite and I had the cooler plugged into the cigarette lighter the whole time.  When we arrived at the park, I plugged the cooler into a power supply and left it.  Even the next day, the things inside were barely cool to the touch.  I ended up removing most of the items and putting them in a regular cooler until they got cold and then put them back into the electric cooler.  It did maintain the temperature of items that were already cold, but seemed to struggle at cooling down warm items.

The adapter that comes with it that allows you to plug the cooler into a 120v outlet has a few problems.  It doesn't hold the 12v connector very tightly and I had to keep checking to make sure it hadn't come unplugged.  Finally, I plugged the 12v cord into the adapter and then taped them together.  That worked pretty well.

I would not recommend this item for camping.  If you have a power supply and items are already cold, it might be okay, but if you are roughing it (without power), it isn't so good.  Coleman recommends you don't leave it plugged into your car's 12v outlet for more than 4 hours or your battery may go dead and 4 hours at a shot isn't enough to keep this cooler cold.
It's great, however, to use in the house for parties and other special events, and it's cheaper than a standard mini-fridge.

title: Coleman 32-oz. Aluminum Bottle(Colors may vary)
description: The Coleman Aluminum Bottle is lightweight and durable, great for hiking, camping, and any activity when you want a beverage handy. Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. title: Coleman 24-Can Party Stacker Portable Cooler
description: You'll be the life of the party when you take along the Coleman 24 Can Party Stacker Cooler to your next event. Not limited to canned drinks, the wide base fits 13 x 9 in. serving dishes, so you can easily pack something for everyone. No matter what you bring, the molded handles will make it easy to get it there. To add even more variety, grab a few coolers in different colors and organize your fun. Use one color to chill the adult drinks, another color for the kids, and keep food warm in yet another, so everyone will know where to find their favorite treats. When the fun is done, the specially designed base fits in the lid of other Party Stacker items, making them simple to stack and store until the next glorious get together.
reviewText: Ice melts fast. Lid isn't insulated.

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText:
When I first opened this and laid it out, the directions made no sense whatsoever. Fortunately I was able to figure it out and get the tent set up in a snap... or so I thought. Then I noticed the roof of the tent was sagging. Although all of the side poles had snapped easily into place, I hadn't been able to get the top one to snap at all. After multiple additional attempts and enlisting ever-stronger people to help me (ending with a guy who's 6'5"), we FINALLY got it to snap into place. When we finally got back home and set it up to air out, the same thing happened--we needed two strong adults playing tug-of-war on the top beam to get it locked. If I had purchased it rather than getting it free for review, I would have returned it.  As it is, I will never take it camping unless I have another adult with me, and/or a second tent as backup.

In case mine was simply defective, here are some other thoughts:
* Lots tent for your money - it's very tall inside and would fit 8 sleeping mats laid out flat on the floor, so it's exceptionally roomy for 3-6 people
* Weatherproofing is OK but not great - we had a couple hours of light rain and although we had a handful of puddles in the tent they were small--a tablespoon here and there rather than drenched. We were at a beach with 90-degree days and 75-degree nights so this wasn't a problem for us, but it would be a concern in more rain, or if camping in colder weather
* Includes a divider to separate the tent into two halves, which is great for privacy while changing. I was too short (5'2") to attach the one at the top of the tent, as I simply couldn't reach that high. But we did okay leaving that one loose / it turned the divider into something more like a stall in a dressing room-- provided ventilation, but you couldn't see over it
* The zippers are mildly annoying. They would snag two to three times every single time we opened or closed the zipper over the entire week of camping **but** it was super easy to unsnag, so this was a mild annoyance rather than a big one
* The tent includes two pockets for hanging small items like car keys or a phone. I had expected zero so this was a welcome surprise, although my other tents have more pockets, and more would have been helpful here too
* Six windows provide good ventilation. They don't unzip all the way--I assume for weather proofing--but they unzip enough to get the job done
* Weighs 26 pounds so it's lighter than most toddlers and easy for one person to carry, you just wouldn't want to use it for backpacking; this is for car camping only
* The tall size means it's a good source of shade when camping in overly sunny areas

Overall, it's a decent tent once set up, but getting the roof to stay put is fiendishly difficult, and more so for a tent that purports to be easy.

title: Coleman 2000019147 Lantern Spark Igniter
description: Lantern Spark Igniter, Attaches To Most Coleman Lanterns, Flints May Be Replaced With Ordinary Lighter Variety, Match Free Lighting, Includes Spare Flint.
reviewText: Will not work on Quickpack Deluxe lanterns. (model 5155 I think)

title: Coleman RoadTrip Swaptop Steel Stove
description: The porcelain-coated cast iron construction is easy to clean at the campsite or at home. The chrome-plated steel wire construction keeps rust at bay, so you'll be able to bring it on your adventures year after year.
reviewText: love it

title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
reviewText: Inside of the sleeping bag is that cheap, itchy material. Good price, but I don't like the sleeping bag lining.

title: Coleman Mallet with Tent Peg Remover
description: Make sure the ground has a firm grip on your tent stakes, and pound them in with a Coleman Mallet with Tent Peg Remover. The double-sided head is made of high-impact ABS plastic. When the fun is done, specially-designed openings on the handle give you the leverage to pull your tent pegs, no matter how hard the ground is.
reviewText: Not great for pounding in stakes, but wonderful for pulling them out.

title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText: Use it to roast coffee outdoors using a whirley pop popcrn popper. Superb in this role as you can place it  sheltered  from wind. Plenty  of BTU's.

title: Coleman Military Style Canteen
description: Keep your drink close and as cold or hot as you want it in a Coleman Military Style Canteen. This polyethylene canteen leaves no metallic taste. The bottle snaps snuggly into the insulated, cotton cover that clips onto the adjustable belt. A strap even connects the lid to the canteen to prevent you from losing the cap.
reviewText: BAD shipping and or delivery.  it rained, it got wet and it stayed wet for days in the box.  I was out of town when it came, so I had to wash it.  It was part of a gift and the receiver loved it, especially the belt that will get very small as needed.  Great way to get kid to drink water more often, but again, it's going to get wet and stay wet.  Bad part, it's plastic.  So, without taking it out of the bag (kid) it is going to get wet and will need to be removed to dry out.  Don't let it get thrown in a box or closet and forgetT

title: Coleman Elite Perfectflow Propane Lantern
description: Reliable, extended-run outdoor illumination can be yours with the Coleman Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 23 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the footed base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather.
reviewText: I love this lantern and the travel case keeps it nice and secure!

title: Coleman Camper’s Toilet Paper, 3 Rolls
description: Take an everyday hygiene necessity on camping trips with Coleman Campers Toilet Paper. Includes 3 rolls of biodegradable, septic-safe 2-ply toilet paper that easily fits in any camping backpack. Each roll contains at least 65 squares of premium tissue and comes in a case that also doubles as a dispenser for added convenience. Compact and convenient, Coleman Campers Toilet Paper lets you take an essential indoor comfort wherever the trail leads.
reviewText:
The pictures, description, etc all clearly state/show that this product comes as three individually packaged rolls in a bag. This was important to me, as I'd planned to split them up between three bags to be kept in my cars. However, the individual rolls are NOT in individual cases/bags, and in fact are just loose in the primary bag.

Yes, they're useful as toilet paper, but only water resistant and durable if kept together in the original packaging. Not what I wanted.

title: Coleman Camp Wagon
description: Put the Coleman Camp Wagon to the test, with its sturdy steel frame and rugged polyester bin that is so convenient for camping, grocery shopping, park or beach. The accordion folding design makes it easy to store when it isn't in use.150 lb. weight capacity..Durable polyester basket..Wipe clean..Measures 38 in L x 20.5 in W x 22.25 in H.Wt. 21.6 lbs..Import..
reviewText: Wheel does not roll. Only used once and was defective out of box. No offer to return product. Terrible. Terrible Purchase.

title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
reviewText:
Good quality tent overall. But...
1. the main selling point; its quick set up; is exaggerated. You can save a minute or two by not fumbling around with tent poles. However you still have to hammer in stakes in the 4 corners as well as the guy lines. Very little time saved there. Also, the tent must be folded up I a very specific way to fit back in the bag. Took me a few tries to figure out the fold up order.
2. If the permanently attached poles break, you may be stuck with a tent that cant be pitched.
3. The built in rain fly is a pain. It is too easily caught in the door zipper. And offers no additional material extending outward from the door like some models.
4. Coleman claims this is a 3 person tent. As usual, that notion is laughable. Yes, you can squeeze 3 adults in. But only if everyone sleeps in a full straightened out position with no movement at all.
5. No bargain on price considering the above limitations. I would have had the same camping experience with my traditional Coleman 3 person tent at about $40 less.

title: Coleman Tent Kit
description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
reviewText: excellent

title: Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case
description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough case helps protect against dents, scratches and broken globes. Inside, a plastic insert lets you customize the size of the space your lantern rests in. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool, place it inside the case, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
reviewText: NO PLACE FOR THE BASE OR TANK.

title: Coleman Quad LED Lantern Special Edition Ultra Bright 190 Lumens, Red
description:
Take the convenience of four lights in one with you with the Coleman Quad LED Lantern. Together the lantern shines 190 ultra-bright lumens for up to 75 hours. During an emergency, pop one of the four lights off the base for a personal flashlight. They recharge once theyre back on the base for hands-free light. The 24 LEDs last a lifetime, so they never need replacing and the lantern runs cool so its always safe to touch, even when its been on for hours. Combining an area light with four portable lanterns in a single clever unit, the Coleman LED Quad lantern is a versatile choice for indoor or outdoor use. The Quad is equipped with four removable LED panels that hold 24 LED lights total. Users can remove any of the four panels and use them independently, such as when needing a portable light for the bathroom or tent, or they can keep all four attached to the lantern and produce 190 total lumens of light. As a result, the Quad is the perfect light for group camping trips and any other occasion where folks might splinter off into smaller groups during the night. Each of the panels is a fully functioning light, with six 5mm white LEDs, a rechargeable NiMH battery, an independent on/off switch, and a top handle for carrying or hanging. The NiMH battery--which recharges when stored in the lantern base--provides up to 1.5 hours of light on its own. The lantern base, meanwhile, requires eight D-cell batteries (not included), which provide an approximate run time of 75 hours for the entire lantern. Other features include a main on/off switch located on top of the lantern, a light range of 26 feet, and a limited five-year warranty. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Best purchase I have made for camping. This lantern is awesome. Each panel will last for about 2 hours of constant use. They are bright enough that I take two panels off to use for light around the campsite, once they start to get dim I pop the other two off that are fully charged and pop the two that are dim on to charge. That give me a cycle of constant light. Great for night time hiking as well. My boys are 6 and 10 and they can get the panels off and on easily. I am thinking of getting a second one just to have another, I don't even need it, just want another one. There is a model that has two larger more powerful panels rather than the 4 smaller ones, I look to purchase that as well at some point.

title: Coleman Kids Quad Chair
description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText:
These chairs are adorable and seem to be well made.  I have to say though that they seem most appropriate for kids aged 4 to 7.

Buyer beware!!  I tried to return two of these chairs and was offered a total refund of $8.66.  What a rip-off.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: I love this when I'm camping. It is sturdy and moderately easy to store. Wish the stove holder was a solid material instead of mesh.

title: Coleman Tent Kit
description: Set up the tent quickly with the steel tent pegs and rubber mallet. Keep it clean for the entire campout with the nesting hand broom and dustpan. When the fun is done, use the tent stake puller to get the pegs out of the ground easily. All four of these important tools fit nicely inside the mesh carry bag for the next adventure.
reviewText: I bought this kit to use in my 4-person tent. The brush and dustpan are very useful, as is the rubber mallet. The included stakes are thicker and stiffer than most stakes that come with tents, but you only get 4 in the package. Finally, the stake puller tool makes campsite takedown effortless. For $12 you get a host of useful items in side a convenient mesh carrying bag.

title: Coleman Sportster II Dual Fuel 1-Burner Stove
description: The fully-adjustable Coleman Sportster II Liquid Dual Fuel Stove is an easy to use, essential addition to your outdoor gear that cooks breakfast, lunch and dinner in any weather on the single-burner. The cooking surface is compact enough to fit inside a pack, but large enough to prepare a trail meal. It gives you the option of using Coleman liquid fuel or unleaded gasoline. This all-season stove is perfect for camping, hunting and even in cases of emergency.Built-in pot support and wind protection.Filter funnel included.1.1 pint fuel capacity.Size: 7.38" x 7.8" x 6.55".Weight: 2 lb..
reviewText: love it. It gets Hot.  It took me a few times to get the lighting procedure correct an achieve the blue flame.  If I would have read the directions first it would not have been a problem.  It comes with a funnel.

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: Right out of the package this thing was dull. I spent a lot of time honing the blade against a sharpening stone. I shouldn't have do that with a brand-new axe. Once sharpened, it quickly minced chunks of 2X4's into kindling. I had a much tougher time chopping through real tree branches that were only a couple of inches thick. The blade bounces off. There's something not right about the balance of this axe. It wobbles in my hand and fails to hit the branch straight on. Also, the blade quickly becomes dull again and needs constant sharpening.

title: Coleman C003 Soft Backpack Cooler
description: Keep your food and snacks cool from the park to the campsite with a Coleman Backpack Cooler. This stylish bag is large enough to hold 28 cans. Extra padding in the back and waist make it even easier to carry over almost any distance. The BPA- and phthalate-free, heat-welded main compartment is perfect for using with ice and the zippered front pocket lets you store extra snacks and small gear. The bungee cord on the front also provides quick access to other items. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
reviewText: keeps things cold and never leaked, very comfortable to wear
